# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The English section comes first, followed by a Japanese translation.
The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## English

### [Unreleased]

#### Fixed
- **Java generic bounds now surface real bound references, and real short type names no longer get suppressed by generic-parameter spelling heuristics (#642, #644)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now indexes Java type-parameter bounds such as `class Demo<T extends Root & Bound>` and `<U extends Wrapper<Root>>`, keeping the bound types visible as `type_reference` edges. At the same time, the old spelling-based generic-parameter filter no longer hides real short names like `X` or `TResult`, so C# declaration positions keep surfacing genuine type names instead of dropping them as presumed generic parameters. Added focused regressions for Java generic bounds, Java nested generic bounds, and short C# type names. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`.
- **C# nested lambda declaration-pattern shadowing stays scoped to the lambda, preserving outer qualified enum-member edges (#562)** — Added a tighter regression for the `values.Any(value => value is Holder RealNs && values.Any(other => other is Holder Other))` shape so the declaration-pattern receiver stays inside the nested lambda and the outer `RealNs.Status.Ready` references remain visible in both extractor-level and CLI exact-name JSON paths. This pins the same shadowing family as the existing lambda and static-lambda regressions, but exercises a deeper lambda nesting shape that better matches the reported false negative. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **C# named tuple return types inside generic arguments now keep their element-name spacing (#652)** — `SymbolExtractor` no longer collapses the separator space between a tuple element type and its element name when it normalizes generic-return-type text, so shapes like `Dictionary<string, (int x, int y)>` and `Task<((int A, int B), string Name)>` keep readable `return_type` values instead of merging tokens into `intx` / `stringName`. Added extractor regressions covering method and property return types with named tuples, plus the existing unnamed tuple case to keep the canonical compact form intact. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`.
- **C# `using static` の同名型パターン回帰を修正 (#685, #691)** — `using static` で import された enum/const 名と同じ短名を持つ real type を、`value is Red` や `case Red =>` のような pattern 位置で取り違えないようにする regression を追加し、top-level と nested の両方で `references` / `inspect` / `impact` が genuine type dependency を落とさないようにしました。Affects: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`.

### [1.16.0] - 2026-04-30

#### Added
- **Migrated CodeIndex to FSL-1.1-ALv2 with explicit integration and competing-use policy docs** — Replaced the repository-level licensing notice with FSL-1.1-ALv2, added canonical `LICENSES/FSL-1.1-ALv2.txt` and `LICENSES/Apache-2.0.txt`, documented non-competing AI/IDE/MCP/CI integration permissions in `INTEGRATION_POLICY.md`, clarified competing-use terms in `COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md`, updated `TRADEMARKS.md`, `README.md`, `src/CodeIndex/CodeIndex.csproj`, `install.sh`, and release/license-policy workflows, and packaged the new policy files with official releases and NuGet output.
- **Installer `--doctor [vX.Y.Z]` network diagnostics with credential-safe proxy env output (#436, #914)** — `install.sh --doctor` now prints the active proxy environment variables (`HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` / `ALL_PROXY` / `NO_PROXY` plus their lowercase siblings) and probes the upstream URLs the installer would hit (the latest-release API endpoint, the release tarball asset, and the `sha256sums.txt` checksums asset) using `curl -sSI`. Each value is routed through a new `redact_proxy_userinfo` helper so URL userinfo such as `http://alice:hunter2@proxy:8080` surfaces as `http://<redacted>@proxy:8080` — keeping the host/port visible for reachability diagnosis while ensuring `--doctor` output stays safe to paste into logs / issues / support transcripts (the doctor is primarily used to diagnose blocked installs and its output is likely to be shared externally). Credential-less proxy URLs and bare-host `NO_PROXY` values are not rewritten, so the redaction does not misleadingly advertise credentials that were never there. Each probe reports its HTTP status, and on `CONNECT tunnel failed, response 403` (`curl` exit 56) the doctor reuses the existing upstream-proxy guidance so users get a single, actionable next step without needing prior network knowledge: the deny is in an upstream proxy/egress policy before TLS, route substitution alone will not fix it, ask the network team to allow-list at least one artifact host path, or point `CDIDX_GITHUB_BASE_URL` / `CDIDX_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL` at a reachable internal mirror. The version argument is optional — when omitted, the doctor reads the bundled `version.json`; when no version is available either, it still runs the API probe and clearly reports the release-asset probes as skipped so a missing version never masquerades as a probe against a bogus `v0.0.0` URL. Does not install anything, never writes outside `/tmp`, and does not short-circuit on the first failure so one network-policy deny does not hide others. Exits 0 when every probe returns 2xx/3xx, exits 1 otherwise. Tests cover the all-200 success path, the CONNECT-tunnel 403 advisory path, explicit-version + `v`-prefix normalization, the `version.json` fallback, the no-version skip path, the HTTP-4xx-reachability-not-confirmed path, the proxy-env surfacing path, the URL-userinfo redaction path (covering `user:password@host`, `user@host`, and SOCKS5 shapes plus the negative case where no credentials are present), the invalid-version-pass-through diagnostic shape, and the dispatcher wiring via a PATH-stubbed `curl` / `uname` shim. Affected: `install.sh`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/InstallScriptTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLOUD_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #436, #914.
- **JavaScript/TypeScript export-surface extraction now handles same-line minified re-exports and CommonJS bindings (#903)** — `SymbolExtractor` now keeps barrel re-export module names tied to the current statement start instead of the first `from` on the line, so same-line minified forms like `export*from './a';export{foo as bar}from './b';export*as ns from './c';` preserve both the `import` row and the exported property surface. CommonJS named export bindings now also recover same-line and multiline parenthesized wrappers correctly, and TypeScript generic-arrow bindings keep their function classification when the right-hand side starts on a later line. Added focused regressions for same-line minified re-exports, same-line CommonJS assignments, multiline CommonJS assignments, parenthesized CommonJS wrappers, and TypeScript generic-arrow CommonJS exports. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #903.

#### Fixed
- **Java JPMS module directives now emit reference graph edges (#883)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now records `requires`, `uses`, and `provides ... with ...` in `module-info.java` as `type_reference` edges, so `references` and other graph queries can see module dependencies again. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for `requires`, `uses`, and `provides` on a module descriptor. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #883.
- **Reference text now lives once per file/line in `reference_lines`, shrinking fresh indexes and keeping legacy DBs readable** — `DbWriter` now normalizes shared line context out of `symbol_references` and stores it in a dedicated `reference_lines` table keyed by `(file_id, line)`. `DbReader` and `DbSymbolReader` now read from the normalized table when it exists, while still falling back to legacy `symbol_references.context` on older databases so existing `.cdidx/codeindex.db` files remain queryable until they are rebuilt. Added regression coverage for deduped storage, cleanup of `reference_lines` on file deletion, and both current-schema and legacy-schema read paths. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbWriter.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DatabaseTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `README.md`.
- **C# switch-expression positional patterns no longer emit phantom `call` edges (#968)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now suppresses the generic call edge when a C# `CallRegex` hit is the head of a positional switch-expression arm, so same-line and split-line forms such as `Point(var x, var y) => 1` and `Point(var c, var d)\n    => 2` keep the real `type_reference` for `Point` without adding a bogus `call Point` row that would pollute `references`, `callers`, `callees`, and `impact`. Added a focused regression that pins the `Point` `type_reference` count and asserts the phantom `call` row is absent. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #968.
- **JS/TS styled-factory gates now reject post-template comparison and division operators (#997)** — `SymbolExtractor` now treats `<`, `>`, and `/` as rejection operators after a real tagged template, so bindings like `styled.div\`...\` < theme`, `styled.div\`...\` > theme`, and `styled.div\`...\` / theme` no longer survive as phantom component symbols. Added focused JavaScript and TypeScript regressions for the post-template operator forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #997.
- **Bare verbatim `inspect` queries now fail closed before reaching caller/callee analysis (#952)** — `inspect` now rejects bare verbatim tokens like `@` up front and `DbReader` also returns empty caller/callee analysis for that input, so direct library calls cannot descend into an invalid query state. Added regressions for the CLI `inspect @ --exact --json` path and direct `DbReader` calls. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #952.
- **Kotlin / Swift / Scala deep nested triple bodies no longer unwind early at 4+ levels (#1002)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now keeps the 3+ deep triple state on a small stack and only exits when the matching opener/close shape is seen, so a 4th nested triple inside Kotlin raw strings, Swift multi-line strings, or Scala interpolated triples cannot terminate the deep mask too early and leak later source as phantom calls. Added focused regression tests for all three languages.
- **Indexing now emits an initial progress bar instead of leaving only `Indexing...` on short or fast runs** — `cdidx index` now prints a `0.0%` progress line as soon as the file list is known, so small or fast full scans still show a real progress bar instead of only the spinner banner. The spinner is paused while that first bar is rendered, and the existing throttled redraws continue to keep progress visible during the run. Added regressions for the initial `0.0%` render and the redirected-output full-scan banner. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConsoleUiTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **Kotlin / Swift / Scala multi-line triple-quoted strings no longer leak phantom call references (#385, #992, #996, #1000, #1001)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now covers three new languages whose `"""..."""` bodies previously slipped past the single-line `StringLiteralRegex` and polluted `references` / `callers` / `callees` / `impact` / `inspect` / MCP `analyze_symbol`: Kotlin `"""..."""` raw strings (preserving `${expr}` interpolation holes so real calls stay visible; bare `$identifier` is masked with the body because a lone identifier cannot be a call), Swift plain `"""..."""` and extended `#"""..."""#` / `##"""..."""##` multi-line strings (with matching `\(expr)` / `\#(expr)` / `\##(expr)` interpolation holes preserved, and same-line extended raw strings `#"..."#` correctly scanned to the matching `"<hashes>` close), and Scala plain `"""..."""` plus interpolator-prefixed `s"""..."""` / `f"""..."""` / `raw"""..."""` forms (only interpolator-prefixed forms recognize `${expr}` holes; plain `"""..."""` has no interpolation and stays fully masked). Each masker tracks nested block comments (Kotlin, Swift, Scala all allow them), skips single-line strings and char literals before entering a triple open, and resumes masking without state confusion across lines. Hole scanners additionally recognize `//` line comments and `/* ... */` block comments inside the hole so a legal `${ /* } */ after() }` / `\( /* ) */ after() )` comment body cannot close the hole prematurely and drop `after()` from the reference graph. Hole scanners also recognize a nested triple-quoted opener (Kotlin `"""...`, Swift `"""...` / `#"""...`, Scala `"""...`) and the nested literal's own interpolation holes, so a `${innerCall()}` inside a Kotlin nested triple, a `\(innerCall())` inside a Swift nested triple, or a `${innerCall()}` inside an interpolator-prefixed Scala nested triple still preserves the real `innerCall()` edge while body chars between holes stay masked — closing the #992 follow-up where the nested body leaked as phantom references and the #996 follow-up where the nested triple's own real calls were silently dropped. Plain Scala nested `"""..."""` (no prefix) stays fully masked because plain Scala triples have no interpolation. The hole scanners also defensively track triple-quoted literals opened 3+ levels deep (i.e. inside the nested triple's own `${...}` / `\(...)` hole): a depth counter masks the deep body through to the matching close so phantom calls cannot leak even at 3+ deep nesting (real calls 4+ levels deep are sacrificed for masking soundness; full stack tracking would be needed to preserve them). The Swift `\(...)` outer hole branch also recognizes `#"..."#` extended raw strings, and a shared `MaskSwiftSingleLineRawString` helper now masks the body chars while preserving any matching `\#(expr)` interpolation hole bodies (so real call edges inside the raw string still reach the reference graph) — closing the #1001 follow-up where the previous full-mask path silently dropped real `\#(...)` call edges. The Swift deep-nesting depth counter also tracks the deep open's hash count and requires the same hash count at the close (closes #1000), so a deep `<N>#"""..."""<N>#` literal cannot exit deep masking at a stray bare `"""` and let later phantom calls leak. Added focused `ReferenceExtractor` regressions for each language covering the phantom-call scope, the interpolation-hole preservation scope, the hole-comment scope, the nested-triple-in-hole phantom scope, the nested-triple inner-hole real-call-preservation scope, the 3-deep nesting masking scope, and the Swift outer-hole hash-delimited raw-string scope. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/StructuralLineMasker.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #385, #992, #996, #1000, #1001.
- **Grouped `callers` / `callees` rows now expose distinct `reference_kinds` instead of collapsing mixed `call` / `subscribe` / `instantiate` sets to one label (#501)** — `DbReader.GetCallers` and `GetCallees` now aggregate `GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT reference_kind)` alongside the existing preferred-kind column, and `CallerResult` / `CalleeResult` expose the sorted `reference_kinds` array plus a `has_mixed_reference_kinds` bool. The scalar `reference_kind` is retained on both caller and callee JSON (back-compat with existing consumers) and carries the preferred summary kind (`instantiate` > `subscribe` > `MIN(call)`); AI clients that need the full picture read `reference_kinds` + `has_mixed_reference_kinds`. Human-readable CLI output joins mixed kinds with `+` (for example `call+subscribe`) in place of the single preferred label, and the reference-kind column widens dynamically to fit the longest label in the batch so mixed rows no longer overrun the neighbouring column. MCP tool descriptions document the new contract. This lets AI clients distinguish pure event subscriptions, pure constructor hits, and real mixed call-and-subscribe containers without chasing a second `--exact` query. Added focused DB and CLI regressions that pin mixed `call` + `subscribe` containers and keep single-kind rows reporting `has_mixed_reference_kinds=false`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #501.
- **Concurrent readers no longer observe torn multi-statement status / map / analyze snapshots (#180)** — `DbReader.GetStatus`, `DbReader.AnalyzeSymbol` (CLI `inspect` / MCP `analyze_symbol`), and `RepoMapBuilder.Build` (CLI `map` / MCP `repo_map`) now each wrap their multi-SELECT body in a single `BEGIN DEFERRED` transaction so every sub-query resolves against the same WAL snapshot. Without this, a writer committing between the `files` and `symbol_references` COUNT(*) statements inside `GetStatus` let concurrent readers observe impossible states such as `files=836, refs=0` against a steady-state 44k-ref index during an incremental update. The deferred transaction acquires only a SHARED lock on the first SELECT, so it does not block other writers; it is committed explicitly at the end to release that lock promptly, and sub-queries that open their own `SqliteDataReader` are scoped in inner blocks so their handles close before `Commit()`. Added three bug-catching `ConcurrencyTests` regressions: `GetStatus` (`refs == files * refsPerFile` invariant), `AnalyzeSymbol` (`references.Count == callers.Count` plus `WorkspaceLatestModified` matches the refs-count state to also exercise `GetWorkspaceFreshness`), and `GetRepoMap` (`latest_modified == workspace_latest_modified` plus the `Entrypoints` set matches the freshness state to also exercise `GetEntrypoints`); each fails without the matching DEFERRED wrap and passes with it. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/RepoMapBuilder.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConcurrencyTests.cs`, `CLAUDE.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `TESTING_GUIDE.md`. Closes #180.
- **Python f-string format specifiers now keep later calls visible to reference extraction (#984)** — The single-line Python f-string masker no longer treats `#` inside an interpolation as a comment boundary, so legal format specifiers such as `f"{value:#x}"` no longer cut off the rest of the line and hide later calls like `real_call()`. Added a regression that pins the format-specifier case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #984.
- **C# positional `case Type(...):` and `is Type(...)` patterns no longer emit phantom `call` edges (#663, #969)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now suppresses the generic call edge when a C# `CallRegex` hit is the head of a positional switch or `is` pattern, so `case Point(var x, var y)` and `value is Point(var x, var y)` keep the real `type_reference` for `Point` without adding bogus `call Point` rows that would pollute `references`, `callers`, `callees`, and `impact`. Added focused regressions that pin the `Point` `type_reference` count and assert the phantom `call` row is absent. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #663, #969.
- **`--max-line-width 0` help and completion text now advertises the no-clamp compatibility mode (#947, #963)** — `cdidx --help`, shell completion, README, and the MCP schema now consistently say that `0` disables clamping so older automation that uses `0` for full-width output can discover the supported escape hatch without reading source. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConsoleUiTests.cs`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #947, #963.
- **SQL exact-name queries now preserve leading `@` while C# verbatim normalization stays C#-only (#651)** — `DbReader` now limits C# verbatim identifier normalization to `--lang csharp` and language-neutral queries, so SQL exact-name lookups like `--lang sql --name @count --exact-name` keep their leading `@` and still match persisted SQL symbols. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #651.
- **Exact-name bare verbatim symbols now return zero instead of failing closed** — `symbols --exact-name --count` and `definition --exact-name --count` now treat a bare verbatim token like `@` as an exact empty query and return a zero-result JSON/count payload instead of a usage error, while non-exact bare verbatim inputs still fail closed. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **C# verbatim normalization now works for name lookups even without `--lang` while SQLite open/backfill validation retry handling remains in place (#651, #954, #962, #971, #972)** — `DbReader` now normalizes C# verbatim identifier spellings for name-based lookups even when `--lang` is omitted, while non-C# queries preserve leading `@` characters. `DbContext` also keeps transient SQLite open retry/backoff behavior for the main connection path and the backfill-fold validation path, with regressions covering CLI, MCP, and connection setup behavior. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DatabaseTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/LegacySchemaMigrationTests.cs`. Closes #651, #954, #962, #971, #972.
- **C# XML-doc attachment now ignores ordinary block-comment shells and declaration-header continuations (#875, #876, #988)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now refuses to open `/**` when it is lexically inside a normal `/* ... */` block comment, and it treats multiline declaration headers as non-attachable until their generic / continuation depth closes. The same guard also recognizes indented C# raw-string closing delimiters before scanning later block comments, so a raw string cannot leave the file stuck in raw-string mode and reopen the `/**` leak path. Added regressions for ordinary block-comment containment plus triple-slash and delimited generic-field-header negatives, along with an indented raw-string / block-comment follow-up and a raw-string close-line regression. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #875, #876, #988.
- **raw-string の閉じ行が後続コメントをマスクしないように修正 (#988)** — `ReferenceExtractor` の raw-string 終端判定を調整し、末尾にセミコロンや空白が付いた閉じ行でも次のコメント行を raw-string 本体として扱わないようにしました。raw-string close-line を直接確認する回帰テストを追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`、`tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`。Closes #988。
- **JavaScript / TypeScript HOC-wrapped and call-result component bindings now emit `function` symbols (#240)** — `SymbolExtractor` previously matched only two JS/TS binding shapes: plain `function Name(...)` declarations and arrow-function bindings of the form `const Name = (...) => ...` (the arrow row's `stopAfterFirstPatternMatch` requires a literal `=>` right after the `=`). Any PascalCase binding whose right-hand side is a call expression, tagged template, or plain identifier — for example `const Wrapped = React.memo(Component)`, `const Box = React.forwardRef(...)`, `const Lazy = React.lazy(() => import('./X'))`, `const Connected = connect(mapState)(MyComponent)`, `const Styled = styled.div` `` `color: red` ``, or `const WithAuth = withAuthentication(Home)` — fell through the regex list entirely and produced zero symbol rows. `definition`, `symbols`, `callers`, `callees`, `inspect`, and `outline` therefore reported nothing for the most common React HOC patterns, even though the bindings were the public API that dependent modules imported and dependent JSX rendered. A new JavaScript row and a matching TypeScript row now capture HOC-wrapped / call-result / tagged-template component bindings. The right-hand side is intentionally restricted to a narrow allowlist of known HOC prefixes — `React.memo(` / `React.forwardRef(` / `React.lazy(` (the `React.` branch is pinned to these three HOC APIs specifically; bare `React.` is NOT accepted, so non-HOC React API calls like `const Theme = React.createContext(null);`, `const Stable = React.useCallback(() => 1, []);`, `const Derived = React.useMemo(...)`, and `const Ref = React.createRef();` are NOT promoted to phantom `function` symbols), `styled.` / `styled(` / `styled` ``\` ``, and the bare calls `connect(` / `memo(` / `forwardRef(` / `lazy(` / `observer(` / `with<PascalCase>(` — combined with a PascalCase gate on the binding name (`[A-Z]\w*`). Ordinary PascalCase or ALL_CAPS constants whose RHS is not a recognised HOC (`const Config = loadConfig();`, `const ENV = process.env.NODE_ENV;`, `const API = 'https://example.com';`) are explicitly NOT promoted to phantom `function` symbols. The allowlist also excludes `= class extends ...`, so a class-expression binding like `const Widget = class extends React.Component {}` produces a single `class Widget` symbol from the synthetic class-expression pass instead of colliding with a duplicate `function Widget` from this row. The TypeScript arrow-function row and the TypeScript HOC row both accept an optional `: <type>` annotation between the binding name and `=`, so explicitly typed bindings land on the correct row: typed HOC bindings such as `const Connected: React.ComponentType<Props> = connect(mapState)(MyComponent)` still capture via the HOC row, and typed arrow bindings whose annotation itself contains a function type (`const Callback: (x: number) => number = (x) => x + 1`) still win the arrow row with `BodyStyle.Brace` because the annotation parser consumes text lazily so the regex backtracks past the `=>` inside the annotation and locks onto the real arrow operator after the real `=`. Both HOC rows are placed **after** the pre-existing arrow-binding row so a capitalized arrow binding (`const Foo = () => { ... }`) is still captured by the arrow pattern first and its `stopAfterFirstPatternMatch` prevents the HOC row from emitting a duplicate `BodyStyle.None` symbol on the same line. `BodyStyle.None` on the HOC row is an intentional trade-off: the RHS body span of an HOC call is not reliably line-trackable from the declaration line alone, and declaration-only visibility into the symbol is still strictly better than dropping the binding entirely. Added `Extract_JavaScript_DetectsHocWrappedComponentBindings` covering `forwardRef` / `memo(named)` / `memo(arrow)` / `lazy` / `connect(...)(...)` / `styled.div` `` ` ` `` / `withAuthentication(...)`; `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternSkipsLowercaseBindings` pinning that `count` / `total` / `config` do not emit phantom `function` symbols; `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternSkipsPascalCaseNonHocConstants` pinning that `Config` / `ENV` / `API` / `Total` are also rejected by the HOC-prefix allowlist; `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternSkipsNonHocReactApiCalls` pinning that non-HOC React APIs such as `React.createContext(...)`, `React.useCallback(...)`, `React.useMemo(...)`, and `React.createRef(...)` produce zero phantom `function` symbols on both the JavaScript and TypeScript rows (the `React.` branch is strict to `memo` / `forwardRef` / `lazy`); `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsGenericTypeArgumentsOnReactHoc` pinning that TypeScript HOC calls with generic type arguments — `React.forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, Props>(...)`, `React.memo<Props>(...)`, `React.lazy<typeof X>(...)`, bare `forwardRef<T>(...)` / `memo<T>(...)` / `lazy<T>(...)` / `connect<State, Dispatch>(...)` / `observer<Props>(...)` / `with<Pascal><T>(...)`, nested-generic forms like `React.memo<Map<string, Props>>(...)` and `React.memo<Record<string, Map<string, Props>>>(...)` (three levels deep), and function-type arguments like `React.memo<(props: Props) => JSX.Element>(...)` — are still captured, so the narrow `React.(memo|forwardRef|lazy)` gate does not drop the extremely common TypeScript `<TypeArgs>(...)` shape (the narrow allowlist on the TypeScript row accepts one optional `<...>` token before the `(`, balancing up to three levels of nested angle brackets and allowing parenthesised segments inside a generic argument via a shared `TypeScriptOptionalHocTypeArgsPattern` constant whose alternation branches start with disjoint character classes to rule out catastrophic backtracking; the JavaScript row intentionally does NOT carry this optional token because JavaScript has no generic syntax and `const Result = memo < Props > (Component);` is a chained comparison / call expression — pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternDoesNotMatchComparisonShape` across `memo` / `forwardRef` / `lazy` / `connect` / `observer` / `with<Pascal>` / `React.memo`); `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternDoesNotCollideWithClassExpressionBinding` pinning that `const Widget = class extends React.Component {}` yields one `class Widget` symbol and no `function Widget`; `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternDoesNotShadowCapitalizedArrow` pinning that `const Foo = () => { ... }` remains a single symbol captured by the arrow row with a brace body range; `Extract_TypeScript_DetectsHocWrappedComponentBindingsWithTypeAnnotation` pinning the TypeScript HOC type-annotation path (`Connected: React.ComponentType<Props>`, `Styled: StyledComponent<'div', Theme>`); and `Extract_TypeScript_TypedArrowBindingPreservesBraceBody` pinning that a function-typed arrow binding (`const Callback: (x: number) => number = (x) => { ... }`) keeps its brace body range on the arrow row and is not shadowed by the HOC row. A follow-up review pass caught two additional codex-review blockers that are rolled into this entry: (1) `TypeScriptOptionalHocTypeArgsPattern` now balances one level of nested parens inside each generic-argument paren segment (`\((?:[^()]|\([^()]*\))*\)` at every angle-bracket nesting level) so real React callback-prop shapes such as `React.memo<(props: { onClick: (x: number) => void }) => JSX.Element>(Box)` still capture. The inner paren branch (`\([^()]*\)`) is disjoint from `[^()]` at its first character (`(` vs not `(`), so the paren balancer remains ReDoS-safe. Pinned by `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsCallbackPropInsideFunctionTypeGeneric`. (2) A new `ShouldSkipJavaScriptTypeScriptStyledFactoryCandidate` post-match gate rejects the styled factory-capture (`const F = styled.div;`) and plain-call (`const F = styled(Component);`) shapes that the HOC regex branch `styled[.(`` ` ``]` otherwise accepts, because neither form produces a rendered component binding on the declaration line. The gate inspects the raw (unmasked) source line because `StructuralLineMasker.MaskJsTsTemplateLiteralContents` blanks template-literal delimiters — including the opening backtick — to space, so the masked `patternMatchLine` cannot distinguish `styled.div` `` ` ``color: red`` ` `` from `styled.div;`. The real tagged-template forms (`styled.div` `` ` ``color: red`` ` ``, `styled(Component)` `` ` ``color: blue`` ` ``, and TypeScript's `styled.div<Props>` `` ` ``color: blue`` ` ``) all carry a backtick on the same raw line and still capture. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRequiresTaggedTemplateForStyled` and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRequiresTaggedTemplateForStyled`. (3) The styled factory-capture / plain-call gate initially read the full raw line with `rawLine.Contains('`')`, so a statement with an unrelated tagged template on the same physical line — for example `const StyledFactory = styled.div; const note = ` `` ` ``not a component`` ` `` `;` — let the backtick from `const note = ...` re-open the gate for the earlier `styled.div;` binding and reintroduced the phantom `function StyledFactory` symbol the gate was meant to reject. The gate now scans only between the match's absolute end position (`lineOffset + match.Index + match.Length`) and the next `;` (or end-of-line), so any backtick that lives on a different statement of the same line is out of scope and cannot undo the rejection. The `lineOffset` argument threads the pattern's per-line offset through so the cursor starts at the match's real column in the original raw line. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsSameLineMultiStatementStyledFactory` and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsSameLineMultiStatementStyledFactory`. (4) The statement-local scan was still pure raw character iteration, so it misinterpreted commented-out or stringified backticks and `;` characters in four symmetric shapes: a line comment whose body contains a backtick (`const F = styled.div // \`not a template\``), a block comment whose body contains a backtick (`const F = styled.div /* \`not a template\` */;`), a plain string literal whose body contains a backtick (`const F = styled.div + "\`";`), and — the mirror failure — a block comment whose body contains a `;` placed before a real tagged template on the same statement (`const F = styled.div /* ; */ \`color:red\`;`). The first three false-accepted phantom `function` symbols for non-template bindings; the fourth false-rejected a real tagged-template binding because the gate treated the comment-internal `;` as the statement terminator. The scanner now walks the raw line with basic JS/TS lexer awareness — `//` breaks out as end-of-statement, `/* ... */` is skipped (including the unclosed tail case, which also falls through as end-of-statement), and `"..."` / `'...'` literals are skipped with minimal escape handling (`\\` consumes the next character) — so only real source-code characters contribute to the accept/reject decision. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternIgnoresStyledBacktickInCommentsAndStrings` and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternIgnoresStyledBacktickInCommentsAndStrings`. (5) The statement-local scanner was still single-line only, so Prettier / dprint formatted styled bindings whose tagged-template backtick sits on the line after `styled.div` / `styled(Component)` — e.g. `const Card = styled.div\n  \`color: red;\`;` and `const Link = styled(Anchor)\n  \`color: blue;\`;` — never found a real-code backtick on the match line and were rejected as factory-capture phantoms. Conversely an initial keyword-allowlist approach to terminating the multi-line scan (`const` / `let` / `var` / ... on the next line triggers ASI) could not detect identifier-start expression statements such as `const X = styled.div\nfoo(\`x\`)` or `const X = styled.div\nawait foo(\`x\`)` — the next line starts with an identifier, not a keyword, so the gate walked into the unrelated expression's backtick and re-accepted the factory-capture binding as a phantom `function StyledFactory`. The gate now walks across physical lines within a bounded lookahead window, carrying block-comment state across line boundaries so a `/* ... */` that straddles lines still masks correctly, and on each continuation line inspects the first non-whitespace non-comment character: only backtick (the tagged template itself) or `.` (member chain such as `.attrs\`...\``) may continue the expression. Any other starting character — identifier-start expression statements (`foo(\`x\`)`), top-level `await`, declaration keywords (`const Y = 5;`), JSX-element / TS-cast starts (`<Foo>{...}`), or any other non-continuation token — is treated as ASI-inserted statement termination and causes the gate to reject, so `const X = styled.div\nfoo(\`x\`)`, `const X = styled.div\nawait foo(\`x\`)`, `const X = styled.div\nconst Y = 5;`, and `const X = styled.div\n<Foo>{\`not a template\`}</Foo>` all stay rejected while `const X = styled.div\n  \`color: red;\`;` is accepted. `<` is intentionally NOT whitelisted as a continuation starter because styled-components generics always appear before the backtick on the same expression, never on a new line — so a continuation `<...>` is always either a JSX element or a TS cast, i.e. a new statement inserted by ASI. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsMultiLineTaggedTemplateContinuation`, `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsMultiLineTaggedTemplateContinuation` (the TS variant additionally pins `MultiLineAnnotated` so a `: ComponentType<Props>` type annotation on the binding name does not confuse the gate), `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsExpressionStatementWithBacktickOnNextStatement`, `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsExpressionStatementWithBacktickOnNextStatement`, `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsJsxElementOnNextStatement`, and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsJsxElementOnNextStatement`. (6) Once the gate walked across physical lines, the inner accept-on-first-backtick step still did not check whether the backtick was actually in tag-head position on the current expression. A binding of the form `const NotStyledMember = styled.div + ` `` ` ``not a styled template`` ` ``;` (or `const NotStyledCall = styled(Component) + ` `` ` ``also not`` ` ``;`, including the typed-annotation variant `const AnnotatedNotStyled: StyledComponent<'div'> = styled.div + ` `` ` ``still not`` ` ``;`) walked through the `+` operator, found the backtick on the same statement, and accepted the candidate as a phantom `function NotStyledMember` / `function NotStyledCall` / `function AnnotatedNotStyled`. The scan now tracks paren / bracket / angle / brace depth so a backtick inside a nested expression (e.g. `.attrs({ ... })`) does not count as the tag head, and rejects the candidate the moment it sees a depth-0 operator character (`+`, `-`, `*`, `%`, `?`, `!`, `&`, `|`, `^`, `=`, `,`, `:`) between the match end and the first backtick. When the regex match already consumed an opening paren (`styled(`, `memo(`, `connect(`, etc.) the depth counter starts at -1 so the upcoming matching `)` restores it to 0 rather than going further negative — Prettier-style multi-line `styled(Component)\n` `` ` ``...`` ` `` shapes are still accepted. The `<` token is also treated as a depth-bumping bracket inside the gate, so even though TypeScript generics like `styled.div<Props>` `` ` ``...`` ` `` are written before the backtick on the same expression they balance to depth 0 by the time the scan reaches the backtick. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsOperatorBetweenStyledAndTemplate` and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsOperatorBetweenStyledAndTemplate`. (7) The depth-tracking gate from (6) accepted on the **first** depth-0 backtick and immediately returned, so a binding of the form `const NotStyledPlusTheme = styled.div` `` ` ``color: red`` ` `` ` + theme;` (or the call-form `const NotStyledCallPlusTheme = styled(Component)` `` ` ``color: blue`` ` `` ` + theme;`, including the typed-annotation variant `const AnnotatedNotStyledPlusTheme: StyledComponent<'div'> = styled.div` `` ` ``color: red`` ` `` ` + theme;`) was promoted to a phantom `function NotStyledPlusTheme` symbol — the gate saw the tag-head backtick, accepted, and never noticed the post-template `+` operator that turns the right-hand side into a binary composition expression. Separately, the gate's lookahead window was capped at 8 physical lines, which is too small for Prettier-formatted bindings whose `.attrs((props) => ({ ... }))` argument object spans more than 10 lines before the backtick — those bindings ran past the window and were silently rejected. The gate now (a) walks across the **entire** template body — including `${ ... }` substitutions and across raw line boundaries with `\\` escape handling — when it sees the depth-0 tag-head backtick, sets a `tagHeadConsumed` flag, and continues scanning so that a depth-0 operator character (`+`, `-`, `*`, `%`, `?`, `!`, `&`, `|`, `^`, `=`, `,`, `:`) appearing **after** the closing backtick still rejects, while a depth-0 `;` after the closing backtick accepts the styled binding cleanly; (b) bumps the lookahead window from 8 to 32 physical lines so realistic Prettier-formatted `.attrs((props) => ({ ... }))` chains still resolve to the trailing tagged template. Two correctness fixes ride along: continuation-line first-character checking is now skipped while `depth > 0`, because lines 2+ of a multi-line nested-expression body (e.g. `field1: props.value1,` inside `.attrs((props) => ({ ... }))`) are expression continuation rather than a new statement and ASI does not insert there; and the arrow function token `=>` is now recognized as a single unit so the `>` does not falsely decrement depth and expose subsequent depth-0 operator characters inside arrow bodies (e.g. `(props) => prop1 ? a : b`) to spurious rejection. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsOperatorAfterStyledTemplate`, `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsOperatorAfterStyledTemplate`, and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsLongAttrsChainBeforeTemplate`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #240.

### [1.15.3] - 2026-04-25

#### Fixed
- Fix the missed version update in version.json.

### [1.15.2] - 2026-04-25

#### Fixed
- **JavaScript / TypeScript line-continuation strings outside template holes no longer leak phantom references (#844, #970)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now keeps top-level JS/TS single- and double-quoted strings alive across physical line boundaries the same way it already handled template-hole continuations, so `identifier(` text inside a backslash-continued string cannot survive into `references`, `callers`, or `impact`. Added regression coverage for JavaScript with CRLF continuation and TypeScript with a normal continued string, plus a for-of scan-buffer guard for continued single-quoted text. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/StructuralLineMasker.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #844. Closes #970.
- **SQL FROM lists now keep later tables after derived-table sources (#942, #974)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats parenthesized derived tables as skippable source-list items, so later comma-separated tables such as `accounts` in `SELECT * FROM (SELECT 1) x, accounts;` remain visible to `references` and graph queries instead of stopping at the derived-table item. The existing table-hint handling from #941 stays covered by the current regressions, and column-alias-list cases are covered by the same source-list path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #942, #974.
- **PowerShell scope-prefixed functions and DSC/workflow declarations now extract correctly (#254, #308)** — `SymbolExtractor` now strips `script:` / `global:` / `local:` / `private:` from `function` / `filter` declaration names, and it also recognizes `configuration`, `workflow`, `using namespace`, and `using assembly` as PowerShell symbols. Added a focused regression that covers the plain and scope-prefixed function forms plus the additional top-level declaration forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #254, #308.
- **C# exact `references` now keep preserved `is`-pattern call rows while still suppressing `case` constant-pattern phantoms (#890)** — The exact C# static-constant pattern suppress path now distinguishes `case` and `is` anchors before removing call rows, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` keeps the real `value is Color.Red or Color.Blue` hit while continuing to drop the same-file phantom from qualified `case Color.Red ...` patterns. Added a CLI regression that pins the `is`-pattern call row in `QueryCommandRunnerTests`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #890.
- **SymbolExtractor exact-duplicate suppression now uses hash-based per-file bookkeeping (#517)** — `AddSymbolRecord` now tracks exact symbol identities and same-line signature occurrences in per-file hash maps instead of rescanning the accumulated list on every append, preserving the same-line restart duplicate guard while avoiding the quadratic hot path. Added a regression that keeps the surviving same-line `Child` row singular in the `#571`-style fixture and a skipped large-fixture smoke test for symbol-volume performance. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/PerformanceTests.cs`. Closes #517.
- **`--max-line-width 0` help and completion text now advertises the no-clamp compatibility mode (#963)** — `cdidx --help`, shell completion, README, and the MCP schema now consistently say that `0` disables clamping so older automation that uses `0` for full-width output can discover the supported escape hatch without reading source. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConsoleUiTests.cs`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **`status` summary freshness now tracks indexed and source timestamps instead of wall-clock age (#928)** — `status` no longer labels a clean index stale just because it was built more than a few minutes ago. The one-line summary now compares the stored `indexed_at` and `latest_modified` timestamps, so a workspace is fresh when the index is at least as new as the source snapshot and stale only when the source is newer or the workspace is dirty. Added regression coverage for both the fresh and stale timestamp cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #928.
- **CommonJS named-export comparisons no longer look like assignments (#856)** — The shared JS/TS named-export regex now requires a real assignment operator, so top-level comparisons such as `module.exports.foo === undefined;`, `exports.bar == null;`, and `module.exports.baz !== 1;` no longer emit fake exported properties. Added JS/TS regressions covering the comparison cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #856.
- **`outline` now computes nested symbol depth from the container chain (#194)** — `outline` output no longer flattens every nested symbol to one level. `DbReader.GetOutline` now derives a per-symbol `Depth` from the stored container chain, human-readable outline output indents by that depth, and the JSON / MCP payloads expose the same field for AI clients. Added regressions for the nested depth chain in both `DbReaderTests` and `QueryCommandRunnerTests`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #194.
- **Single-line interpolated string references are preserved again** — `ReferenceExtractor` now restores call expressions inside single-line interpolated strings / template literals for C#, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python before call scanning, so `references` / `callers` no longer drop hits like `Run()` in `$"..."`, `` `...${run()}` ``, or `f"..."`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`.
- **Python f-strings now keep interpolation calls visible to reference extraction (#264)** — `PrepareLine()` now masks single-line Python f-string text while preserving `{...}` expression contents, so calls such as `run()` inside `f"value = {run()}"` stay visible to the reference extractor instead of being erased with the surrounding literal text. Added a regression that pins the call edge inside the interpolation. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #264.
- **Raw FTS query syntax errors now report usage errors instead of database errors (#187)** — `search --fts` now classifies FTS5 parser failures such as `tokio::spawn`, `AND OR`, and unterminated quoted input as query-authoring mistakes instead of database failures. The CLI prints a dedicated `FTS5 query syntax` message, returns `CommandExitCodes.UsageError`, and adds a hint that explains when `:` is being treated as an FTS5 column qualifier. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #187.
- **SQLite connection setup now retries transient busy/locked errors during concurrent reads** — `DbContext` now retries a few times when opening a connection or registering SQLite functions fails with a transient `SQLITE_BUSY` / `SQLITE_LOCKED` condition, which makes the concurrent-read test path more stable on Linux CI without changing the observable database contract. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`.
- **Long single-line query payloads can now be left untruncated with `--max-line-width 0` (#830)** — `search`, `references`, `find`, `excerpt`, and `inspect` now accept `--max-line-width 0` as an explicit full-line mode, so long Markdown or audit lines can be reviewed without `...(+N)...` elision when the caller needs the entire physical line. The MCP counterparts now expose the same zero-as-full-line contract through `maxLineWidth: 0`, `LineWidthFormatter` preserves the full line in that mode, and the CLI help/docs now describe the no-truncation escape hatch. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/LineWidthFormatter.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SearchSnippetFormatterTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #830.
- **C# exact `references` now preserve type-alias hits even when `using static` makes the same name look like a constant pattern (#715)** — `DbReader` now resolves active C# `using Alias = ...` scopes, including `global using`, through the current namespace before deciding whether they point at a real type, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` keeps `using Red = RealTypes.Red;` hits without mistaking namespace aliases for type aliases. Added regression coverage for the CLI exact-name/count path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #715.
- **C# `using static` same-name type references now survive alias and nested scope checks (#693)** — `references` / `inspect` / `impact` now keep alias-scoped real types visible even when a `using static` constant shares the same short name, and C# same-file constant-pattern suppression no longer hides nested real types that shadow imported enum members. Added direct regressions for the alias-scoped `Probe.Shapes.Red` case and the nested `Outer.Red` case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #693.
- **JS/TS の行継続文字列が template hole の外でも phantom reference を漏らさないよう修正 (#844, #970)** — `StructuralLineMasker` が、template-hole の継続で既に行っていたのと同じように、物理行をまたぐ JS/TS の single-quote / double-quote 文字列を保持するようになり、backslash 継続された文字列内の `identifier(` テキストが `references` / `callers` / `impact` に残らなくなりました。JavaScript の CRLF 継続、TypeScript の通常継続文字列、そして継続された single-quoted テキスト向けの for-of scan-buffer guard を固定する regression も追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/StructuralLineMasker.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`。Closes #844。Closes #970。
- **SQL の FROM list で derived-table の後続テーブルが消えないよう修正 (#942, #974)** — `ReferenceExtractor` が parenthesized な derived table をスキップ可能な source-list 項目として扱うようになり、`SELECT * FROM (SELECT 1) x, accounts;` のような形でも、後続の comma-separated テーブル `accounts` が `references` と graph query から途中で切れずに残るようになりました。既存の #941 の table-hint 処理は現行の regression で引き続きカバーされ、column-alias-list ケースも同じ source-list 経路で扱われます。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`。Closes #942、Closes #974。
- **PowerShell の scope-prefixed function と DSC/workflow declaration を正しく抽出するよう修正 (#254, #308)** — `SymbolExtractor` が `function` / `filter` の宣言名から `script:` / `global:` / `local:` / `private:` を除去するようになり、さらに `configuration`、`workflow`、`using namespace`、`using assembly` も PowerShell symbol として認識するようになりました。通常形と scope-prefixed 形の function、追加の top-level declaration 形をカバーする focused regression も追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`。Closes #254, Closes #308。
- **C# exact `references` が `is` パターンの call row を保持しつつ `case` constant-pattern phantom を抑止するよう修正 (#890)** — C# の exact static-constant pattern suppress 経路が、call row を消す前に `case` と `is` の anchor を区別するようになり、`references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` は `value is Color.Red or Color.Blue` の本物の hit を保持したまま、qualified な `case Color.Red ...` パターン由来の同一ファイル phantom を引き続き落とせるようになりました。`is` パターンの call row を固定する CLI regression も追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`。Closes #890。
- **SymbolExtractor の exact-duplicate suppression を hash ベースの per-file bookkeeping に変更 (#517)** — `AddSymbolRecord` が、same-line signature occurrence を毎回 accumulated list から再走査する代わりに、per-file の hash map で exact symbol identity を追跡するようになり、same-line restart duplicate guard を維持しながら quadratic な hot path を避けられるようになりました。`#571` 風 fixture で surviving した same-line `Child` row が 1 件だけ残る regression と、symbol volume 性能向けの skipped large-fixture smoke test も追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/PerformanceTests.cs`。Closes #517。
- **`status` の summary freshness が wall-clock age ではなく indexed / source timestamp を見るよう修正 (#928)** — `status` が、clean な index を「数分以上前に作られた」というだけで stale 扱いしなくなりました。1 行 summary は保存済みの `indexed_at` と `latest_modified` を比較するため、workspace は index が source snapshot と同等以上に新しいとき fresh になり、source の方が新しいか workspace が dirty のときだけ stale になります。fresh / stale 両方の timestamp ケースを固定する regression も追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`。Closes #928。
- **CommonJS の named-export comparison が assignment に見えないよう修正 (#856)** — 共有 JS/TS named-export regex が本物の assignment operator を要求するようになり、`module.exports.foo === undefined;`、`exports.bar == null;`、`module.exports.baz !== 1;` のような top-level comparison が fake な exported property を出さなくなりました。comparison ケースを固定する JS/TS regression も追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`。Closes #856。
- **`outline` が container chain から nested symbol depth を計算するよう修正 (#194)** — `outline` 出力が、すべての nested symbol を 1 段に平坦化しなくなりました。`DbReader.GetOutline` が保存済みの container chain から各 symbol の `Depth` を導出し、人間向け outline 出力はその depth でインデントし、JSON / MCP payload でも AI client 向けに同じ field を公開します。nested depth chain を固定する regression を `DbReaderTests` と `QueryCommandRunnerTests` の両方に追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`。Closes #194。
- **Python f-string が interpolation call を reference extraction で見失わないよう修正 (#264)** — `PrepareLine()` が single-line Python f-string の text をマスクしつつ、`{...}` の expression 内容は保持するようになり、`f"value = {run()}"` の中の `run()` のような call が、周囲の literal text と一緒に消されず reference extractor に見えるようになりました。interpolation 内の call edge を固定する regression も追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`。Closes #264。
- **生の FTS クエリエラーを database error ではなく usage error として報告するよう修正 (#187)** — `search --fts` が、`tokio::spawn`、`AND OR`、閉じていない quoted input などの FTS5 parser failure を query authoring の誤りとして扱うようになり、database failure と誤認しなくなりました。CLI は専用の `FTS5 query syntax` メッセージを出し、`CommandExitCodes.UsageError` を返し、`:` が FTS5 の column qualifier として解釈されるケースの hint も追加します。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`。Closes #187。
- **長い 1 行の query payload を `--max-line-width 0` で untruncated にできるよう修正 (#830)** — `search`、`references`、`find`、`excerpt`、`inspect` が `--max-line-width 0` を明示的な full-line mode として受け付けるようになり、長い Markdown や audit line も `...(+N)...` で省略されず、その physical line 全体を必要とする呼び出しでそのまま確認できるようになりました。MCP 側も `maxLineWidth: 0` で同じ zero-as-full-line 契約を公開し、`LineWidthFormatter` もその mode で full line を保持し、CLI の help / docs も no-truncation の escape hatch を説明します。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Database/LineWidthFormatter.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SearchSnippetFormatterTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`。Closes #830。
- **Bare verbatim `inspect` query が caller/callee analysis に入る前に fail closed するよう修正 (#952)** — `inspect` が bare verbatim token の `@` を事前に拒否し、`DbReader` もその入力に対して空の caller/callee analysis を返すようになったため、直接の library call が無効な query state へ降りていくことがなくなりました。CLI の `inspect @ --exact --json` 経路と direct `DbReader` 呼び出しを固定する regression を追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`。Closes #952。
- **C# exact `references` で `using static` によって同名が定数パターンに見えても type alias の参照を残すよう修正 (#715)** — `DbReader` は、C# の `using Alias = ...` スコープを現在の namespace を通して解決してから実体が本物の type か判定するようになり、`references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` が `using Red = RealTypes.Red;` のヒットを保持しつつ namespace alias を type alias と誤認しなくなりました。CLI の exact-name/count 経路の regression を追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`、`tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`。Closes #715。
- **C# の `using static` で同名型参照が alias / nested スコープ判定で落ちないよう修正 (#693)** — `references` / `inspect` / `impact` が、`using static` の constant と同じ短い名前を持つ alias-scoped な実型を保持するようになり、さらに C# の同一ファイル内 constant-pattern 抑止でも、import 済み enum member を shadow する nested の実型を隠さなくなりました。Probe.Shapes.Red の alias ケースと Outer.Red の nested ケースを固定する直接 regression を追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`、`src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`、`tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`、`tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`、`tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`。Closes #693。
- **C# の positional `case Type(...):` と `is Type(...)` パターンが phantom な `call` edge を出さないよう修正 (#663)** — `ReferenceExtractor` は、C# の `CallRegex` が拾ったヒットが switch の positional pattern や `is` pattern の head だった場合に generic な call edge を抑止するようになり、`case Point(var x, var y)` と `value is Point(var x, var y)` は `Point` の本物の `type_reference` を残したまま、`references` / `callers` / `callees` / `impact` を汚す `call Point` 行を追加しなくなりました。`Point` の `type_reference` 件数と phantom `call` 行の不在を固定する focused な regression も追加しました。対象: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`、`tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`。Closes #663。

### [1.15.1] - 2026-04-24

#### Fixed
- **Release artifacts now ship license notices and the installer preserves them** — `create-release` now copies `LICENSE`, `COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md`, and `TRADEMARKS.md` into the published payload, and `install.sh` installs those notices when they are present alongside the binary.

### [1.15.0] - 2026-04-24

#### Changed
- **Hardened licensing and distribution policy against unauthorized productized forks** — Replaced the package and repository licensing metadata with PolyForm Perimeter 1.0.0, added commercial-use and trademark policy documents, embedded the policy files into NuGet packages and release payloads, gated official release publishing to the canonical repository, added `cdidx license`, and documented legacy package handling. Affected: `LICENSE`, `COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md`, `TRADEMARKS.md`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `src/CodeIndex/CodeIndex.csproj`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ProgramRunner.cs`, `.github/workflows/release.yml`, `.github/workflows/license-policy.yml`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/LicensePolicyTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConsoleUiTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ProgramCliTests.cs`.

#### Fixed
- **SQL qualified non-exact graph readers no longer widen to longer qualifier chains (#766)** — `references` / `callers` / `impact` now keep qualified SQL lookups schema-scoped when they resolve a recorded call column, so `sales.proc_name` no longer matches `archive.sales.proc_name` through substring containment. The column-aware non-exact path now requires the same normalized segment count and an exact qualified-name match, matching the existing documentation contract and keeping longer qualifier chains out of qualified results. Added a regression that proves `sales.proc_name` stays empty while `archive.sales.proc_name` still resolves. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SqlNameResolver.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #766.
- **Graph-query JSON zero results now emit envelopes for non-exact misses (#798)** — `references`, `callers`, and `callees` now always print the structured zero-result JSON payload when `--json` is set, even when the graph table exists and the query misses without `--exact`. This keeps the machine-readable contract consistent with the exact and degraded zero-result paths and preserves freshness metadata for AI clients. Added CLI regressions for the non-exact zero-result path on all three commands. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #798.
- **Java annotation members with array defaults no longer look brace-bodied (#793)** — `FindJavaBraceRange` now treats `default { ... }` after a Java parameter list as an annotation default value instead of a real member body, so `value()` in `@interface Tags { String[] value() default {"a", "b"}; int age(); } class C {}` keeps `body_start_line` / `body_end_line` null and its signature closes at the semicolon while later same-line members still survive. Added a focused regression for the annotation member, sibling member, and trailing same-line class. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #793.
- **SQL `FROM` lists now keep later source tables instead of dropping comma-separated tails (#802)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now captures comma-separated `FROM` sources as a single SQL source-list match, so later tables such as `accounts` in `SELECT * FROM users, accounts;`, `accounts` in `SELECT * FROM users WITH (NOLOCK), accounts;`, and `events` in `SELECT * FROM public.logs l, [archive].[events] e;` remain visible to `references`, `inspect`, and graph-driven navigation instead of disappearing after the first comma. Added focused extractor regressions and kept the existing `JOIN` / `APPLY` / `DELETE ... USING` source handling intact. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #802.
- **C# enum member collisions no longer hide unused symbols (#701, #931, #932)** — `unused` now keeps C# enum members with duplicate names conservative instead of treating them as authoritative unused hits, so a reference to `Color.None` no longer suppresses `Status.None` in the same workspace. The collision guard now uses qualified container identity when available and applies the caller's path / test scope to peer checks, so out-of-scope duplicates do not distort scoped `unused` results. Unique enum members still appear normally. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #701, #931, #932.
- **Regression coverage for C# switch-expression arm type references after guarded or relational earlier arms (#851, #852)** — Added extractor and built-CLI regressions that lock later switch-expression arm type heads as visible after an earlier `when`-guarded arm, after earlier recursive patterns with relational `<` / `>` operators, and after later generic arm heads such as `Wrapper<Point, Shape>`. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #851, #852.
- **SQL `DELETE ... USING` now keeps temp sources after a comma on the temp-table path, including mixed source lists and `using`-substring table names (#792)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer treats the comma tail after `DELETE FROM ... USING #staging_a AS a, #staging_b AS b`, the implicit-alias form `#staging_a a, #staging_b b`, or mixed lists such as `DELETE FROM audit_log USING reusing_data, #staging_b AS b` as a MySQL `# comment`, so later `#temp` sources continue to surface as `reference` edges instead of disappearing from the graph. Added regression coverage for both alias spellings plus the mixed regular-table / temp-table list, including a regular table whose name contains `using` as a substring. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #792.
- **Option-looking literals can now be passed as query text (#923)** — Query commands with positional query terms now accept `--query <query>` / `--query=<query>` and a one-token `-- <query>` escape, so users can search for literals such as `--path` without the parser treating them as CLI options. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`.
- **Positional query literals that begin with `--` are now accepted in search (#799)** — `search` now treats the first unknown dash-prefixed positional token as the query text instead of rejecting it as an unsupported option, so quoted literals such as `--open-reports` can be searched directly alongside the existing `--query` and `-- <query>` escapes. Added parser and CLI regressions for the direct positional case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #799.
- **Regression coverage now pins SQL double-quoted dotted `EXEC` / `EXECUTE` procedure targets (#722)** — Added extractor and DB-reader regressions for single double-quoted SQL identifiers that contain dots, such as `EXEC "sales.fn_Target";` and `EXECUTE "sales.fn_Target";`, so exact `references`, `callers`, and `impact` continue to distinguish that one quoted identifier from a real schema-qualified `sales.fn_Target`. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #722.
- **Java same-line record and brace-valued annotation extraction no longer drops inline members or emits phantom record-header functions (#760, #761, #762, #801)** — `SymbolExtractor` now keeps Java same-line type-body scans anchored inside the actual class/record/enum body instead of reopening the header for later regex rows, and the Java method pattern no longer treats `record R(...)` headers as `function R` rows with `return_type = "record"`. Java compact-constructor extraction now advances across same-line record-body siblings, so `int first() { ... } public R { ... }`, `static int y; public R { ... }`, and `public R { ... } int later() { ... }` keep both the compact constructor and neighboring members visible. Added focused regressions for brace-literal annotation arguments on same-line methods, records, and enum-member bodies, one-line records with inline methods and compact constructors, compact constructors after same-line sibling statements, and same-line members after compact constructors. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #760, #761, #762, #801.
- **SQL `EXEC` / `EXECUTE` / `CALL` no longer misindex MySQL `# comment` tails as T-SQL temp stored procedures (#656)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now gates bare `#tempProc` / `##tempProc` after `EXEC` / `EXECUTE` / `CALL` on same-file T-SQL establishment (prior `CREATE TABLE #x`, `SELECT ... INTO #x`, `#x` mutation target, or the newly recognized `CREATE PROCEDURE|PROC|FUNCTION #x` with optional `OR REPLACE|ALTER` / `TEMP|TEMPORARY`). Without that evidence the proc-call extractor now drops the would-be call edge so MySQL / MariaDB line comments such as `CALL #commented_out;` / `EXEC #tempProc;` / `EXECUTE #tempExecute;` stop polluting `references` / `callers` / `callees` / `impact` / MCP graph tools, while quoted forms such as `EXEC [#tempProc];` and `` EXEC `#backtickProc`; `` bypass the gate because bracket / backtick quoting is unambiguously an identifier. `ShouldTreatHashAsSqlCommentCore` also allows `PROCEDURE` / `PROC` / `FUNCTION` / `CALL` to keep `#name` as an identifier so the establishment tracker can register temp routines before their later proc-call sites run through the gate, and `CanSqlStatementEstablishTempObject` recognizes `SqlCreateTempRoutineRegex` so semicolon-less `CREATE PROCEDURE|FUNCTION #x` lines still flush the temp name before the next-line `EXEC` / `CALL` runs through the gate. Added focused extractor regressions for the MySQL comment false-positive, the established-temp-routine positive path (including the semicolon-less `CREATE PROCEDURE|FUNCTION` shape), and the quoted-form bypass. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #656.
- **JavaScript/TypeScript symbol extraction now surfaces barrel re-exports and CommonJS export bindings across commented, multiline, minified, namespace, shorthand, spread-safe, non-identifier-key-safe, parenthesized, type-only-star, import-attributes, and TS-generic-arrow forms (#226, #848, #849, #858, #861, #864, #870, #871, #872, #885, #888, #895, #896, #905, #906, #908, #909, #916, #917, #921, #922)** — `SymbolExtractor` now emits symbol rows for JS/TS re-export surfaces such as `export * from`, `export * as ns from`, `export { foo as bar } from`, `export type { User } from`, `export type * from`, and `export type * as ns from`, including multiline star / namespace-star forms, standalone `export` + `{ ... } from` layouts, trailing-comment cases, same-line and multiline import-attributes suffixes such as `with { type: 'json' }` / `assert { type: 'json' }`, and minified valid syntax such as `export*from` / `export{foo as bar}from`. Those re-exports now preserve the source-module `import` rows as well as the exported property surface without letting comment text hijack the recovered module specifier after lex sanitization. It also surfaces direct CommonJS named exports like `module.exports.foo = function () {}`, `module.exports.bar = () => {}`, `module.exports.fn = <T>(x: T) => x`, `module.exports.foo = <T>(\n  value: T\n) => value`, `module.exports.foo = <T extends (...args: any[]) => number>(value: T) => value`, and `exports.baz = value` even when the right-hand side starts on a following line, is wrapped in same-line or multiline leading parentheses, appears inside top-level conditional blocks, uses async / constrained TypeScript generic-arrow headers, or puts the function body's opening `{` on a later line, while keeping identifier prefixes like `functionCall()` / `classyThing` classified as ordinary property values rather than false function/class exports. Exported object-literal alias and shorthand properties such as `module.exports = { foo: inner }` and `module.exports = { foo, bar }` stay visible without misclassifying top-level spread entries or quoted/computed key values as exported properties. This keeps `symbols`, `definition`, and related symbol-oriented queries from silently missing or corrupting public JS/TS API surfaces that were already exported but previously invisible to the index. Added focused extractor regressions for commented, multiline, minified, type-only-star, and import-attributes barrel re-exports, namespace re-exports, newline-separated `export` + `{ ... } from` forms, multiline named import-attribute layouts, re-export source-module import rows, comment-safe module recovery, same-line, multiline, parenthesized, constrained, async, and TS generic-arrow CommonJS named export assignments, brace-body range preservation, top-level conditional CommonJS exports, prefix-safe CommonJS RHS classification, exported object-literal alias/shorthand properties, quoted/computed object keys, and spread entries. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #226, #848, #849, #858, #861, #864, #870, #871, #872, #885, #888, #895, #896, #905, #906, #908, #909, #916, #917, #921, #922.

### [1.14.0] - 2026-04-24

#### Added
- **Regression coverage for C# metadata-target `signature` column fallback on legacy / read-only DBs (#431)** — `LegacySchemaMigrationTests` now pins the deps / impact path that exercises `BuildMetadataTargetKindExpr`'s `name LIKE '%Attribute'` fallback when `_symbolColumns` does not contain `signature`. The new fixture seeds a normal C# attribute class + `[Audit]` reference, drops the `signature` column, reopens the DB read-only (so `TryMigrateForRead` cannot re-add it), and asserts that `GetFileDependencies` still resolves the `User.cs → Audit.cs` edge via the attribute-suffix alias and `AnalyzeImpact` still surfaces `User.cs` as a heuristic consumer — closing the #431 test-coverage gap so any future regression that removes the column-guarded fallback surfaces as `SqliteException: no such column: s.signature` in CI rather than at query time. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/LegacySchemaMigrationTests.cs`. Closes #431.

#### Fixed
- **`type_reference` kind contract now consistent across `references` / `callers` / `callees` (#444)** — `QueryCommandRunner.AllValidReferenceKinds` now includes the compile-time `type_reference` edge kind emitted by `ReferenceExtractor` for C#/Java base lists, declaration types, generic constraints, `throws`, `is`/`as`/`instanceof`, and XML-doc `cref`, so `cdidx references <name> --kind type_reference` no longer prints the contradictory `unknown reference kind` warning while still returning hits. A new `CallGraphOnlyReferenceKinds` set is used by `callers` / `callees` so those commands only accept `call` / `instantiate` / `subscribe`, and `TryRejectMetadataKindForGraphCommand` has been generalized to `TryRejectNonCallGraphKindForGraphCommand` (plus the MCP `IsNonCallGraphReferenceKind` / `BuildNonCallGraphKindRejectionMessage` helpers) so CLI and MCP both reject `--kind type_reference` (alongside `attribute` / `annotation`) on `callers` / `callees` with `CommandExitCodes.UsageError` and a hint redirecting users to `references --kind type_reference`. MCP `references` / `callers` / `callees` tool descriptions and `kind` schemas are updated to match the new contract. Added focused CLI and MCP regressions covering `references --kind type_reference` success, `callers` / `callees --kind type_reference` rejection, and the existing metadata-kind rejection paths. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #444.
- **Fold-only readiness degradation now surfaces concrete remediation in `index`/`status` instead of a generic warning (#290, #893)** — when `fold_ready=false` is the only remaining degraded readiness bit, human-readable `index` output now prints a fold-specific warning with an in-place `cdidx backfill-fold --db ...` hint and a full-rebuild fallback instead of only pointing users at `status --json`. `status --json` exposes `fold_ready_reason` plus fold-only `degraded_reason`, `recommended_action`, and `alternative_action`, and `index --json` now mirrors those same fold-specific remediation fields so post-index AI flows do not need a second `status` round-trip just to decide how to repair the DB. The one-line status summary now marks fold-only degradation as `DEGRADED`. Added focused CLI regressions for the fold-only `index` warning, incremental/update `index --json`, and both JSON/human `status` paths. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #290, #893.
- **Fold-only remediation commands now normalize read-only `file:` DB URIs back to writable paths instead of echoing failing commands (#854, #867, #892)** — `status --json` no longer reuses explicit read-only `file:` DB URIs verbatim in `recommended_action` / `alternative_action`; absolute `file:///...?...immutable=1` / `mode=ro` inputs and relative `file:codeindex.db?...` inputs now both point at the writable filesystem path for the same DB, `cdidx index <project> --db file:...immutable=1` is rejected up front with an actionable error instead of reaching an unhandled SQLite readonly-write exception, and `index --dry-run --db file:...immutable=1` remains allowed because dry-run does not mutate the database. Added focused CLI regressions for the absolute/relative read-only `status` remediation paths, the direct `index --db file:...immutable=1` error path, and the `--dry-run` exemption. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/DbPathResolver.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbPathResolverTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #854, #867, #892.
