- **Exact graph-supported definition probe no longer crashes on legacy/read-only DBs missing `container_kind` (#493)** — `DbSymbolReader.GetExactGraphSupportedDefinitionLanguage` now routes its C# enum-container filter through the shared `GetSymbolColumnSql("container_kind", "''")` compatibility helper, so the probe stays on the backward-compatible read-schema contract when `TryMigrateForRead()` cannot add the `container_kind` column in place (for example on a read-only filesystem). This keeps exact `inspect`, `references`, `callers`, `callees`, MCP `analyze_symbol`, and the graph-support hints used by `impact` working on older indexes instead of hard-failing with `SQLite Error 1: 'no such column: s.container_kind'`. Added a focused regression that seeds a legacy DB, drops `container_kind`, bypasses read-time migration, and verifies the exact graph-support probe still resolves the symbol language. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #493.
- **Java `module-info.java` now emits JPMS symbols and keeps filtered repo maps coherent (#305, #889, #898, #899, #911)** — `SymbolExtractor` now indexes Java module declarations as `namespace` symbols and surfaces `requires`, `exports`, `opens`, `uses`, and `provides` directives as `import` rows, including multiline `to` / `with` lists, comment-separated `requires` statements, and Allman brace-line directives, while `RepoMapBuilder` now uses the declared module name for both `module-info.java` and sibling Java source files that belong to the same JPMS module even when `map --path` filters the descriptor file out. `symbols`, `definition`, `outline`, and `map` no longer treat JPMS descriptors as empty or anonymous structure, and `map` now surfaces a coherent module boundary for the whole Java module across both full and path-filtered views. Added focused Java extractor and map regressions and updated the language-pattern reference. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/RepoMapBuilder.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #305, #889, #898, #899, #911.
- **JS/TS no-paren constructor calls now reach the reference graph (#295)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now emits `instantiate` edges for JavaScript / TypeScript zero-argument constructor sites that legally omit `()`, such as `new Foo;`, `new Date;`, qualified targets like `new Demo.Provider;`, and one-level generic TypeScript forms like `new Box<number>;`. Added focused extractor regressions for the reported JS and TS shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #295.
- **C# exact `references` now expands multiline `using static` constant-pattern context until the real anchor is visible (#863)** — `DbReader` now grows the read-path context window for C# exact `type_reference` suppression until a `case`/`is` anchor becomes visible instead of stopping after a tiny fixed lookback, so long multiline `switch` constant patterns no longer leak the final `Red:` head in exact JSON or count results. Added focused CLI regressions for the long multiline `case` pattern and its `--count` path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #863.
- **C# exact qualified constant-pattern rows now stay suppressed end-to-end (#869)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer emits phantom `call` rows for same-file enum/member sites such as `case Color.Red or Color.Blue:`, and `DbReader` still keeps the exact-name suppress path pinned to the resolved qualifier instead of unrelated same-name types like `class Red {}`. Added built-CLI regressions that lock exact `references`, `--count`, and `callers` at zero for the reported single-file repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #869.
- **C# exact using-static suppress now survives blank/comment separators and long multiline `case` chains (#862, #873)** — `DbReader` no longer relies on a fixed reconstruction window when exact-name C# `using static` suppression checks multiline `case` / `or` chains. It now reconstructs anchor-aware context across blank lines, treats comment-only lines as trivia, and keeps walking backward until it rediscovers the real pattern anchor, so blank-line splits, comment-separated heads, and long multiline constant patterns no longer leak phantom `type_reference` rows in exact `references` queries. Added built-CLI regressions that keep the non-exact ambiguous rows visible while asserting exact `references` and `--count` stay at zero for all three reported shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #862. Closes #873.
- **C# multiline `not` continuation lines now keep real type-pattern heads (#891)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer clears pending multiline C# `is` / `case` type-pattern state just because the next continuation line contains only `not`. Valid forms such as `value is` / `not` / `Point` and `case` / `not` / `Point:` now keep their real `type_reference` rows instead of disappearing before the head line is reached. Added extractor and exact-name CLI regressions for both shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #891.
- **C# non-type `case` labels no longer arm multiline type-pattern carry (#857)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now only enters the pending multiline C# `case`-type state when the `case` line actually ends where a later type head can continue. Relational and other non-type labels such as `case > 0:` and `case not > 0:` therefore no longer cause the next-line call token to be mis-recorded as a phantom `type_reference`. Added extractor and CLI regressions that pin the negative `case > 0:` shape alongside the multiline positive cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #857.
- **C# multiline type-pattern carry now survives trivia-only separator lines (#850)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer flushes pending C# multiline `is` / `case` pattern state just because a structurally masked comment-only line collapses to whitespace, so comment-separated heads such as `value is` + `// comment` + `Point`, `case` + `// comment` + `Point:`, and multiline `using static` constant patterns now keep their `type_reference` rows. Added extractor and CLI regressions for the comment-separated `is`, `case`, and `using static` shapes, and updated the developer guide to document the trivia-only carry boundary. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #850.
- **C# multiline `case` labels now keep cross-line first heads and later logical heads without reviving qualified constant-pattern noise (#843, #747)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now carries C# `case` labels through the same pending type-pattern state machine used by multiline `is` / `as`, so both `case` + next-line first heads (for example `case` / `Point:`) and cross-line logical continuations (for example `case Point or` / `Shape:`) preserve their `type_reference` rows. The new carry path also leaves multiline using-static ambiguous constant heads visible for read-time filtering, while keeping qualified constant/member labels such as `case Color.Red or` / `Color.Blue:` suppressed so no phantom `type_reference` rows come back. Added extractor plus CLI regressions for the multiline `case` type, logical, and constant-pattern shapes, and updated the developer guide to match the new C# pattern boundary. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #843. Closes #747.
- **C# switch-expression arm type patterns now index the arm heads as compile-time dependencies (#732)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now scans C# switch-expression arm heads through the same type-vs-constant discrimination already used for `case` labels, so arm heads such as `Point =>`, `Point or Shape =>`, and generic heads like `Wrapper<Point, Shape> =>` emit `type_reference` rows while constant/member arms such as `Color.Red =>` stay on the non-type path. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for bare type heads, multi-head logical patterns, generic type heads, and the constant-member control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #732.
- **C# multi-line expression-bodied members now keep full signatures and same-line terminator-line siblings (#835, #836)** — `SymbolExtractor` now reuses the shared semicolon-terminated declaration extent scan for multi-line expression-bodied C# members, so stored signatures include continuation lines through the terminating `;` instead of truncating at the declaration line. The same extent data now also narrows the #779 continuation-line body guard: later-line field/property candidates remain suppressed while they are still inside the already-emitted member body, but real same-line siblings that start after the terminating `;` on the final line are allowed through again. Added focused extractor plus CLI `symbols` / `outline` regressions for the reported `Match(...) => value is` + `Red; public int X;` shape. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #835. Closes #836.
- **C# multiline constant patterns no longer emit phantom property symbols or lose file-level references (#779)** — `SymbolExtractor` now stops reinterpreting later lines inside an already-emitted C# member body as field/property declarations, so continued constant-pattern heads such as `value is` followed by later-line `Red / or / Red` no longer leak bogus `property Red` symbols into `symbols`, `outline`, or `inspect`. `ReferenceExtractor` also carries C# `is` / `as` pattern heads across line breaks, preserving the real `type_reference` rows for multiline heads instead of dropping them when the first type token starts on the next line. Added focused extractor regressions plus CLI `inspect` / `references` coverage for the reported shape. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #779.
- **JS/TS template-hole line-continuation strings now keep hole state and real calls (#433)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now tracks JavaScript/TypeScript single- and double-quoted strings that continue across physical lines inside `${...}` holes, so a continued string body can no longer close the hole early with `}` or leak phantom references, and the real call that follows the string stays visible to the reference graph. Added JS/TS regression coverage for the continued-string repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/StructuralLineMasker.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #433.
- **C# nullable tuple suffixes no longer hide the trailing real reference after parenthesized query clauses (#616)** — the reverse scan that classifies `?` before `select(`/`group(`/`orderby(` as a nullable cast/type-test suffix now also balances tuple parentheses, so legal casts such as `value as (int Left, int Right)?` no longer stop at `)` and wrongly suppress the later `Status.Ready` reference outside the query. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for the nullable tuple suffix alongside the existing nullable scalar/array coverage. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #616.
- **C# exact `definition` / `inspect` no longer promote interpolated call sites into fake function definitions (#790)** — `SymbolExtractor` now rejects C# symbol candidates whose start column lives inside nested interpolation code instead of root code, so exact `definition` / `inspect` return only the real declaration for `DescribeState`-style methods even when multi-line interpolated log strings contain call-site text that previously stitched into phantom `function` rows. Added extractor and CLI regressions for the reported multi-line interpolation repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #790.
- **Zero-result `unused` / `hotspots` now suppress stale SQL trust warnings when the active symbol kind cannot match any SQL symbols (#829)** — zero-result symbol-oriented summaries no longer inherit `sql_graph_contract_ready=false` merely because the workspace contains stale SQL graph rows somewhere else. `unused` / `unused --count`, `hotspots`, grouped hotspots, and the MCP `unused_symbols` / `symbol_hotspots` tools now narrow zero-result SQL trust relevance against the filtered SQL symbol candidate set, so queries such as `--kind interface` or `--kind class` stay clean on mixed C# + SQL indexes when no SQL symbol could possibly contribute. Added focused CLI/MCP regressions for the non-SQL-kind zero-result paths. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Closes #829.
- **CLI `unused --count` zero-result summaries now keep stale SQL trust warnings in mixed SQL-backed scopes (#828)** — `unused --json --count` no longer infers `sql_graph_contract_ready` relevance solely from counted rows, so a zero-result mixed SQL-backed scope now preserves the stale SQL trust fields instead of returning a false-clean summary. Added a focused CLI regression for the zero-result count-only path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #828.
- **Zero-result SQL-backed dependency/unused summaries no longer drop stale trust warnings (#824)** — zero-result CLI/MCP `deps`, `hotspots`, and `unused` payloads now keep `sql_graph_contract_ready` / `sql_graph_contract_degraded_reason` when the active scope itself is SQL-backed, instead of inferring `Relevant=false` from an empty visible result set. This keeps stale SQL graph-contract DBs from returning falsely clean zero-result dependency or unused answers in JSON/MCP while preserving the existing non-SQL clean paths. Added focused CLI/MCP regressions for zero-result SQL-scope fixtures. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Closes #824.
- **Mixed-language SQL trust-signal narrowing now also stays accurate for CLI `--count` summaries (#822, #823)** — `references` / `callers` / `callees --count` and `unused --count` no longer decide `sql_graph_contract_ready` relevance from a single preview row or from `--lang sql` alone. The count readers now report whether the aggregated scope actually includes any SQL-backed rows/symbols, so mixed C# + SQL counts keep the degraded SQL trust fields whenever SQL contributes to the returned aggregate, while pure non-SQL counts remain clean on the same stale mixed-language index. Added focused CLI regressions for mixed count-only fixtures plus `DbReader` compatibility touch-ups for the updated count summary shape. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #822, #823.
- **SQL graph trust signals no longer degrade unrelated non-SQL query results in mixed-language indexes (#818)** — CLI/MCP graph and dependency payloads now emit `sql_graph_contract_ready` / `sql_graph_contract_degraded_reason` only when SQL-backed rows actually contribute to the result or exact-resolution path. Mixed-language indexes that contain stale SQL graph rows no longer mark pure C# / JS / etc. `callers` / `callees` / `references` / `inspect` / `impact` / `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots` output as degraded just because SQL files exist elsewhere in scope. Added focused CLI/MCP regressions for mixed SQL+C# fixtures so stale SQL contract rows stay visible for SQL queries while pure C# queries remain clean. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #818.
- **SQL graph readiness now degrades explicitly until unchanged SQL rows are rebuilt under the current contract (#786)** — Added a dedicated `sql_graph_contract_version` stamp plus `sql_graph_contract_ready` / `sql_graph_contract_degraded_reason` status fields so CLI status, index summaries, exact graph queries, `deps`, `unused`, `hotspots`, and `impact`, plus MCP status/dependency/impact tools, all tell users and AI clients when a DB still carries pre-fix SQL graph rows. Normal full scans now force unchanged SQL files to be reindexed when that contract stamp is stale, restoring current SQL call-column semantics before restamping readiness. Added CLI/MCP regressions for degraded status exposure plus the full-scan upgrade path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbWriter.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #786.
- **SQL graph queries now keep schema-qualified readers schema-scoped across exact and non-exact lookups, while definition-oriented readers continue to normalize quoted qualified names consistently (#296, #710, #713, #716, #721, #724, #728, #738, #742, #744, #745, #746, #754, #758, #763, #765, #769, #770, #775, #778, #781, #796, #797, #803)** — SQL definition names remain stored with their schema/package qualifier, but graph/dependency readers now reconstruct each SQL reference identity from the stored source-line context plus recorded call column before comparing it to definitions. That keeps qualified `references` / `callers` / `impact` lookups on the matching schema even when one line contains multiple qualified calls or the lookup is non-exact, while still letting genuinely unqualified SQL source sites fall back to the bare leaf. When a row already has a recorded call column, downstream readers now refuse to whole-line-upgrade that row to a different qualified token later on the line, so comment text, string literals, or a second qualified call no longer make `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots` misattribute the first unqualified call. Aggregate `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots` readers now also reuse the same raw-leaf fallback guard as the exact SQL graph readers, so a bare `EXEC fn_Target` still counts toward `dbo.fn_Target` when no real one-segment `fn_Target` definition exists in scope instead of disappearing from dependency, hotspot, or unused summaries. The SQL no-parens call scanner now also preserves ANSI / PostgreSQL double-quoted call targets such as `CALL "sales"."proc_name"` / `EXEC "dbo"."fn_Target"` instead of stripping them as string literals, so exact/unqualified SQL graph lookups can see those real call edges without emitting phantom qualifier targets. The same SQL no-parens scan now masks earlier same-line string literals and inline block comments with equal-length spaces before matching, so recorded call columns stay aligned with raw source lines and qualified `references` / `callers` still reach `dbo.fn_Target` in shapes such as `SELECT 'prefix'; EXEC dbo.fn_Target;` or `SELECT /*note*/ 1; EXEC dbo.fn_Target;`. Exact SQL graph/dependency readers now also preserve the resolved segment count, so a quoted single identifier containing a dot such as `"sales.fn_Target"` no longer collides with the real qualified name `sales.fn_Target` across exact `references` / `callers` / `impact` and aggregate `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots`. Quoted single-identifier containers that merely contain dots, such as `"sales.Caller"`, also no longer donate a fake schema qualifier when an unqualified `EXEC fn_Target` falls back to the leaf target, so aggregate readers prefer the real exact one-segment target over `sales.fn_Target`. Qualified SQL `callees` queries likewise no longer widen to sibling callers such as `dbo.Caller` when the query is `sales.Caller`, and exact SQL `callees` now normalize bracketed T-SQL caller names such as `[sales].[fn_Target]` before matching so `callees sales.fn_Target --exact` reaches the same outgoing edges as the raw bracketed form. Non-exact CLI JSON `references` / `callers` / `callees` results now keep the SQL graph contract trust fields, while MCP `definition` stops surfacing those graph-contract signals because definition results do not depend on SQL graph readiness. SQL symbol extraction now applies the same shared qualified-identifier pattern across the remaining CREATE / ALTER DDL variants, so supported definitions such as `sales . seq_orders`, `[sales] . [syn_demo]`, and `"sales" . "ext_demo"` also stay fully qualified instead of truncating to the first segment. The same position-aware path still preserves real same-line calls such as `CREATE PROCEDURE sales.fn_Target AS EXEC dbo.fn_Target;`, keeps `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots` aligned with qualified calls, accepts optional whitespace around qualified-name dots, preserves segment-count-aware exact definition matching, and keeps Unicode exact SQL lookups on the folded path. Added DB/CLI/MCP regressions covering non-exact qualified `references` / `callers` / `callees`, same-line multi-call isolation, downstream mixed-line/comment/string isolation, same-line string/block-comment column preservation, exact qualified schema isolation, bracketed exact callee normalization, quoted-definition normalization, double-quoted SQL call targets, quoted-single-identifier exact/aggregate separation, quoted-container leaf fallback, aggregate bare-call fallback, SQL graph trust-field exposure, Unicode exact matching, multi-name exact symbol counts, and spaced-dot SQL symbol extraction. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SqlNameResolver.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #296, #710, #713, #716, #721, #724, #728, #738, #742, #744, #745, #746, #754, #758, #763, #765, #769, #770, #775, #778, #781, #796, #797, #803.
- **JavaScript / TypeScript symbol extraction: class-field arrows, top-level generators, and object-literal method shorthand (#212)** — The JS/TS extractor now emits `function` symbols for three constructs that previously fell through to the field-initializer state machine or missed the top-level regex entirely: (1) class-field arrow properties such as `handleClick = () => { }` and the TypeScript annotated form `handleClick = (): void => { }` (including TS generic arrow fields like `transform = <T>(x: T): T => { ... }`), which are captured by a new arrow-specific header parser that walks optional visibility / field-type annotations, the `=` assignment, optional `async`, generic parameters, parameter list or single identifier, optional return-type annotation, and the `=> {` brace-opening; (2) top-level generator functions `function* gen()` / `async function* gen()` — the top-level regex now allows `function\s*\*` between `function` and the name; (3) object-literal method shorthand inside `const obj = { ... }`, `let obj = { ... }`, `var obj = { ... }`, `export const obj = { ... }`, `module.exports = { ... }`, `module.exports.name = { ... }`, and `exports.name = { ... }` — a new object-literal scan-target collector routes these through the existing class-body scanner so `get foo()`, `set foo(v)`, `*bar()`, `async baz()`, and plain `qux()` shorthand members surface as function symbols with `container_kind="object"` and `container_name` set to the binding alias. Plain value fields (`value = 42`, `count: 42`, `items = [1, 2, 3]`) remain correctly classified as non-functions. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`.
- **C# const fields with tuple / nullable / array / qualified return types no longer disappear from symbol extraction (#346)** — `SymbolExtractor` now reuses the shared `CSharpTypePattern` (which understands balanced tuple parentheses, nullable suffixes, array ranks, and qualified names) on the C# `const` field row instead of a hand-rolled `[\w@?.<>\[\],:]+` character class. This keeps declarations such as `public const (int, int) Pair = (1, 2);`, `const (int a, int b) NamedPair = (3, 4);`, `const (int, int)? MaybePair = null;`, `const (int, int)[] PairArray = ...;`, and `const global::System.Int32 Qualified = 42;` visible in `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline` while the matching phantom `function const` guard from #496 still holds. Added a focused regression that locks the five tuple-return shapes and the baseline `int` control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #346.
- **C# nested `new enum` declarations are no longer dropped by symbol extraction (#353)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts the `new` member-hiding modifier in `CSharpEnumDeclarationRegex` alongside the existing `file` modifier, so nested `public new enum Kind { A }` / `new public enum KindReversed { A }` / `public new enum KindByte : byte { A }` forms inside derived types are captured as `enum` symbols instead of silently disappearing from `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline`. Added focused regressions for the enum fix and for the already-supported `new delegate` companion, pinning both `public new delegate` and reversed `new public delegate` modifier orders. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #353.
- **Strengthened #362 regression coverage for C# `new Qualified.Type()` phantom-method shapes** — The core fix (excluding `new` from the explicit-interface return-type slot) shipped in v1.13.0. This adds a broader end-to-end regression fixture (`Svc` with `ChainedNew` / `DiscardNew` / `UseNew` / `BraceInitNew` methods emitting `new System.Text.StringBuilder().Append(...).ToString()`, `_ = new System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex(...)`, `new System.Net.Http.HttpClient().Dispose()`, and brace-initializer `new Outer.Inner { A = 1 }.Consume()` forms, plus an `Outer.Inner.Consume()` container chain and a sibling `Consumer : System.IDisposable` with a real `void System.IDisposable.Dispose() { }`) and pins the full five-axis `Outer` -> `Inner` -> `Consume` chain (total `Outer`=1, total `Inner`=1, `Inner` under `Outer`=1, total `Consume`=1, `Consume` under `Inner`=1) alongside a kind-agnostic `Name == "new"` absence guard, so future widening of the explicit-interface pattern cannot silently regress the dot-chain-qualifier / brace-initializer / discard / chained-call shapes. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Re-runs #362.
- **C# body-less same-line members no longer absorb the next file line's brace range (#515)** — `SymbolExtractor.FindCSharpBraceRange` now clamps body-less function-like declarations (`void M();`, ctor signatures, etc.) at the in-line `;` once a parameter list `(` has been seen, instead of relying solely on the trailing `EndsWith(';')` fallback. That fallback never fired for same-line shapes whose physical line ends with the enclosing type's `}` (`{ int P { get; } void M(); }` or even single-member `{ void M(); }`), so the scanner kept reading into the next file line and attributed the next class's `{ ... }` body range — and container — to the body-less symbol. The new clamp uses a `sawOpenParen` guard so it stays inert when `absoluteStartColumn` arrives in collapsed-generic / attribute-stripped match-line space and slides the scan onto an earlier sibling's `;` (e.g. `event ... E;` ahead of a same-line `class Wrapped<T>`). Added a focused regression that pins range / body / container metadata for the property→method, single-member, and method→property orderings plus the `property + event` baseline. Verified on the locally built binary against the issue repros: M now reports `end_line=1` with null body for `interface I { void M(); }` and `end_line=2` for `interface J { int P { get; } void M(); }`. Also confirmed that the originally-reported #514 generic-event/interface/delegate semicolon loss no longer reproduces (the trailing `;` is preserved by the existing collapsed-to-raw signature translator), so this commit also closes #514. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #514, #515.
- **C# top-level statement callers now surface as a synthetic `<top-level>` graph caller (#242)** — `DbReader` no longer drops C# call-graph rows whose `symbol_references.container_name` is null because the call site lives in top-level statements. `callers`, `callers --count`, `impact`, `inspect`, and other caller-backed read paths now surface those rows as `function <top-level>` instead of returning a false zero-caller result for `Program.cs`-style entrypoints. Added focused DB and CLI regressions for the top-level `Run();` repro while preserving the existing outline contract that executable top-level bodies are not indexed as symbols. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #242.
- **Java Allman-style compact constructors are indexed again (#791)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts compact-constructor headers whose opening `{` moves to the next physical line, so records such as `public record Sample(int value)\n{\n    public Sample\n    {\n        ...\n    }\n}` no longer lose the constructor symbol. Added a focused Java extractor regression that pins the Allman-style constructor symbol, body range, and record component. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #791.
- **Java same-line body-less members now keep later same-line siblings under the same container (#788)** — `SymbolExtractor` now restarts Java body-less declaration scanning from the next sibling after a top-level `;` and clamps same-line Java signatures to that statement terminator. This keeps later members such as `int age();` in `@interface Tags { String[] value(); int age(); } class C {}` indexed as members of `Tags` instead of swallowing `} class C {}` into the signature or dropping the sibling entirely. Added a focused Java extractor regression that pins both member signatures and the trailing same-line `class C`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #788.
- **Comma-separated SQL temp names no longer disappear into MySQL-style `# comment` stripping (#789)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now iterates every captured temp target in repeated `TRUNCATE TABLE #a, #b` lists and recognizes comma-separated SQL identifier lists before a `#temp` as source/target continuations rather than `# comment` tails. This keeps later temp names visible in both `TRUNCATE TABLE #a, #b; SELECT * FROM #b;` and `DELETE FROM audit_log USING #a, #b` without regressing real MySQL hash comments. Added focused extractor regressions for both the truncation-establish path and the `DELETE ... USING` source list. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #789.
- **C# exact `references` with `using static` now page past large suppressed prefixes without unstable same-line ordering (#735)** — `DbReader` now keeps the `using static` constant-pattern suppression on the exact C# read path while switching the filtered search loop from restart-with-larger-limit to `OFFSET`-based paging with expanding page sizes and a total-order exact-reference sort (`line`, `column_number`, `reference_kind`, `symbol_name`), so a real later hit is no longer lost after 65,536 suppressed enum-member pattern rows or at same-line page boundaries. Added `DbReader` regressions that seed 65,537 suppressed rows ahead of a later real call and pin same-line exact-reference ordering by column. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #735.
- **Java same-line `@interface` members and annotated record headers keep correct body ranges (#784)** — `SymbolExtractor` now keeps Java same-line nested-member scanning on its Java-specific brace path instead of reusing the C# same-line brace-end scanner, treats top-level `;` as a body-less Java declaration terminator even when a later sibling `}` appears on the same line, and ignores annotation-array braces while locating the first real class/record body opener for compact-constructor discovery. This prevents `@interface Tags { String[] value(); } class C {}` from stretching `value()` into the next declaration and keeps compact record constructors indexed for headers such as `record Sample(@Tags({"a", "b"}) int value) { public Sample { ... } }`. Added focused Java extractor regressions for same-line `@interface` members, enum anonymous-member bodies, and annotation-array record headers. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #784.
- **SQL `TRUNCATE TABLE #temp` now establishes later temp reads (#768)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats temp-table truncation as an establishing write in the same temp-object gating path used by `INSERT` / `UPDATE` / `DELETE` / `SELECT ... INTO` / `CREATE TABLE`, so `TRUNCATE TABLE #temp; SELECT * FROM #temp;` no longer drops the later read. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for both direct and line-end-comment truncation forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #768.
- **SQL bare identifiers keep non-ASCII unquoted names while also allowing `$` (#764)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now keeps the SQL bare-identifier contract Unicode-aware while still allowing PostgreSQL-style `$` characters, so unquoted names such as `ユーザー` continue to index on source/target/proc-call paths instead of disappearing from exact-name graph queries. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions that lock both `reference` and `call` counts for non-ASCII SQL identifiers. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #764.
- **SQL line-end target-prefix carry now covers general target forms, and bare `$` identifiers stay whole on source/target paths (#726, #759)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now keeps unfinished target-introducer prefixes such as `INSERT INTO -- comment`, `UPDATE -- comment`, `DELETE FROM -- comment`, `TRUNCATE TABLE -- comment`, and `CREATE TABLE -- comment` on the same SQL statement path, so the next-line object name is still indexed as a `reference`, temp-target establishment still reaches later reads, and `INSERT INTO ... (` does not regress into a phantom `call` on the target identifier. The shared bare SQL identifier contract now also accepts PostgreSQL-style `$` characters (for example `my$table`) across SQL source/target readers, target-call suppression, and exact-name lookups, preventing truncation to the prefix before `$`. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for both behaviors. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #726, #759.
- **SQL source/target references now cover ordinary identifiers and temp tables without leaking `SELECT ... INTO` variables, overloaded `USING` clauses, or SQL string/comment bodies into the graph (#284, #638, #639, #648, #649, #653, #657, #660, #664, #665, #679, #681, #684, #689, #690, #694, #695, #696, #697, #698, #707, #708, #711, #712, #718, #729, #739, #740, #741, #750, #752, #753)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now emits SQL `reference` rows for named source/target forms such as `FROM`, `JOIN`, `INSERT INTO`, `UPDATE`, `TRUNCATE TABLE`, `DELETE FROM`, `DELETE ... USING`, `MERGE [INTO]`, and `MERGE ... USING`, recognizes T-SQL `#temp` / `##temp` identifiers in target paths plus later statement-order reads of temp objects established by earlier `INSERT INTO #temp`, `INSERT TOP (...) INTO #temp`, `UPDATE #temp`, `DELETE FROM #temp`, `SELECT ... INTO #temp`, multiline `SELECT ... INTO #temp`, `CREATE TABLE #temp`, and `MERGE [INTO] #temp` statements, keeps `EXEC`/`CALL` plus table-valued functions on the `call` path (including bracketed/backtick/double-quoted `FROM ... fn(...)` shapes), skips modifier keywords such as `SELECT|INSERT|UPDATE|MERGE|DELETE TOP (...)`, `ONLY`, and `LATERAL` before resolving the real source/target object, and advances temp state across both same-line `;`-separated statements and later semicolon-less statement boundaries including top-level `SET`, `DECLARE`, `DO`, and `BEGIN`. It skips the `MERGE ... WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET ...` action-clause false positive while keeping quoted `[SET]` targets valid, suppresses the old `INSERT INTO ... (` phantom `call` edge plus `SELECT|INSERT|UPDATE|MERGE|DELETE TOP (...)` phantom `call TOP`, no longer treats unrelated PostgreSQL `USING` clauses such as `CREATE INDEX ... USING btree (...)` as source/call edges, keeps real SQL expression calls such as `ALTER TABLE ... USING lower(name)` on the `call` path, keeps SQL Server `MERGE target USING source` forms with or without optional `INTO` including temp-table targets, keeps same-line and multiline SQL Server target hints such as `MERGE INTO audit_log WITH (INDEX(ix), HOLDLOCK) AS t USING staging_log ...`, `MERGE INTO #audit_log` followed by a next-line `WITH (...) AS t`, and unfinished temp/source/hint prefixes split by a line-end `-- comment` such as `SELECT ... INTO -- comment`, `DELETE ... USING src, -- comment`, and `MERGE ... WITH (INDEX(...), -- comment` on the same SQL continuation path, preserves multiline `DELETE ... USING` / `MERGE ... USING` source continuations even when the previous line ended with a `-- comment`, restores PostgreSQL `DELETE ... USING` source references without reopening the DDL `USING` false positives, handles `TRUNCATE TABLE ONLY public.users` plus comma-separated target lists like `TRUNCATE TABLE audit_log, archived_log` as real targets instead of phantom `ONLY` or missing later targets, and keeps those `TRUNCATE` / `DELETE ... USING` paths canonicalized to the terminal object name only so qualified or quoted-qualified forms do not emit duplicate or mangled exact-name graph rows. It treats double-quoted SQL text as object names only when the introducer keyword itself sits outside the quoted region so dynamic SQL strings no longer leak phantom `reference users` or falsely establish later `#temp` reads, blanks multiline single-quoted string bodies plus multiline `/* ... */` comments and PostgreSQL `$$...$$` / `$tag$...$tag$` bodies before source/target scanning so non-code SQL text cannot leak phantom `reference phantom` rows or phantom temp establishment, closes same-line dollar-quoted bodies at the first real delimiter so later same-line statements like `...$$; SELECT * FROM users; ...` are still scanned, preserves MySQL/MariaDB backslash-escaped single quotes inside SQL string scanning so `\'` literals also stay opaque, and still strips MySQL/MariaDB `# comment` tails so `CALL #commented_out` / `FROM #commented_source` do not leak phantom edges. Added focused SQL regression fixtures covering CTE reuse, view/table references, stored procedures, TVFs, quoted TVFs, temp tables, `MERGE ... USING`, optional-`INTO` SQL Server `MERGE` forms, `MERGE [INTO] #temp` forms, `DELETE ... USING`, overloaded-`USING` DDL controls, real `USING <expr>` call preservation, `MERGE ... USING` target hints, multiline temp-merge target hints, unfinished line-end comment continuations, `MERGE ... UPDATE SET` controls, same-line temp statement ordering, semicolon-less temp statement boundaries, `SET` / `DECLARE` temp reads, SQL source/target modifiers, `TRUNCATE TABLE ONLY` / multi-target lists, qualified / quoted-qualified canonicalization guards, dynamic-SQL double-quote guards, dynamic-SQL temp-establishment guards, multiline single-quoted-string guards, multiline block-comment guards, PostgreSQL dollar-quoted-body guards, same-line dollar-quoted-body boundaries, escaped single-quote guards, multiline temp-table materialization, ANSI quoted identifiers, MySQL `# comment` controls, and the `SELECT ... INTO variable` guard, and documented the narrowed SQL graph pattern in the developer guide. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #284, #638, #639, #648, #649, #653, #657, #660, #664, #665, #679, #681, #684, #689, #690, #694, #695, #696, #697, #698, #707, #708, #711, #712, #718, #729, #741, #750, #752, #753.
- **SQL semicolon-less temp-state flushing now recognizes top-level `IF` and `WHILE` boundaries (#757)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats top-level `IF` / `WHILE` as semicolon-less SQL statement starts for temp-object carry flushing, so `SELECT ... INTO #temp` immediately followed by `IF ... SELECT * FROM #temp` or `WHILE ... SELECT * FROM #temp` preserves the later `#temp` read instead of keeping only the establish-side target reference. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for both control-flow forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #757.
- **C# methods with generic attributes on type parameters no longer disappear from symbol extraction (#347)** — `SymbolExtractor` now shares a nested-angle-aware C# method type-parameter matcher across the regular and explicit-interface method rows, so declarations such as `public void M<[GenAttr<int>] U>(U u)`, `public void N<[GenAttr<int>, GenAttr<string>] U>(U u)`, `public void P<[GenAttr<(int, int)>] U>(U u)`, and `void IFoo.Run<[GenAttr<int>] T>(T value)` remain visible in `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline` instead of being silently dropped by a shallow `<[^>]+>` match. Added focused regression coverage for ordinary and explicit-interface methods. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #347.
- **C# phantom local `const` and qualified call-site function symbols are now filtered from symbol extraction (#496)** — `SymbolExtractor` now applies the existing column-aware C# type-body gate to field-like `function` rows (`const` / `static readonly`) so local declarations such as `const string content = "hello";` no longer leak into `symbols`, `definition`, or `outline`. It also rejects C# declaration candidates whose captured return-type fragment ends in an operator/contextual suffix, preventing qualified call arguments like `elapsed < TimeSpan.FromSeconds(10)` from surfacing phantom `function FromSeconds` rows. Added a focused regression that locks both repros. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #496.
- **C# verbatim-identifier return types no longer get dropped by the #496 suffix guard (#782)** — `HasInvalidCSharpReturnTypeSuffix` now recognizes real C# verbatim identifiers before rejecting contextual-keyword tails, so declarations such as `public @new Make() => new @new();` remain visible in `symbols` and `outline` while the `elapsed < TimeSpan.FromSeconds(...)` phantom-call-site filter stays intact. Added a focused regression for the verbatim return-type repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #782.
- **C# contextual-keyword type names like `await` / `yield` no longer get dropped by the #496 suffix guard (#785)** — `HasInvalidCSharpReturnTypeSuffix` now keeps legal contextual-identifier type names while still rejecting truly invalid statement/operator tails, so members such as `public await MakeAwait()` and `public yield MakeYield()` remain visible in `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline`. Added a focused regression covering both contextual-keyword return types. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #785.
- **C# parenthesized LINQ clause guard no longer mistakes local `const` identifiers for cast targets (#624)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats typed local `const` declarations as in-scope value names for the cast-close disambiguation path, so parenthesized `orderby` expressions such as `(READY)` and `(Select)` no longer suppress the real trailing `select(...)` clause solely because the identifier casing looks type-like. Real casts like `(CustomType)select(items)` still keep the clause blocked. Added focused extractor and CLI `references --exact-name --json` regressions for the uppercase-constant repro, and the existing keyword-named-constant regression now passes against the built binary again. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #624.
- **Installer jq-version detection test now uses a real PATH stub instead of a bash function shim (#774)** — `InstallScriptTests.ResolveVersion_UsesJqWhenAvailable` now creates an executable `jq` stub under a temporary directory and prepends that directory to `PATH`, so CI reliably exercises the `command -v jq` branch without depending on bash function lookup details or the runner's preinstalled tools. This keeps the installer's jq-preferring path covered while avoiding spurious stderr on GitHub Actions. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/InstallScriptTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #774.
- **Java same-line annotations, enum-constant body overrides, and record compact constructors now extract correctly (#221, #751, #755)** — `SymbolExtractor` now strips leading same-line Java annotations with the existing lexer-aware scanner instead of a flat `[^)]*` regex prefix, so declarations such as `@Label(")") public int broken()`, `@SuppressWarnings({"unchecked"}) public int first() { ... }`, and annotated `record` headers survive string/comment/paren/brace edge cases. The extractor also emits record compact constructors in both same-line (`public Range { ... }`) and Allman-style (`public Range` followed by next-line `{`) layouts, preserves anonymous enum-member body ranges so overrides like `ADD { @Override public int apply(...) { ... } }` attach to the enum constant container, keeps same-line Java brace-bodied siblings separate (`int first() { ... } int second() { ... }`) instead of swallowing later methods into the first signature, and now restarts same-line Java scanning after enum constants so methods inside `A { @Override int f() { ... } int g() { ... } }` also reach the symbol table. Added focused Java regressions for the reported repros plus the enum-member body range contract. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #221. Closes #751. Closes #755.
- **C# `unused` now reports truly unused enum members instead of degrading the result (#488)** — `unused`, `unused --count`, and MCP `unused_symbols` no longer exclude C# enum-member rows once qualified enum-member access edges are indexed, and the stale `graph_degraded=true` / `unsupported_symbol_kind=enum_member` metadata has been removed. Added DB, CLI, and MCP regressions that keep referenced enum members out while returning genuinely unused members such as `Green`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Closes #488.
- **Test suite no longer serializes every class assembly-wide (#528)** — Removed the assembly-level xUnit `DisableTestParallelization` gate so independent test classes can run concurrently, while keeping process-global hazards serialized by assigning environment-variable and debug-console tests to the existing non-parallel `SQLite pool sensitive` collection. Updated the testing guide to document the new parallel-by-default contract plus the required collection/console-lock rules. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/TestAssembly.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/GitHubIssueReporterTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbDebugTests.cs`, `TESTING_GUIDE.md`. Closes #528.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer indexes plain `//` comments as real type dependencies (#646)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now requires an actual C# XML-doc comment line before it emits `type_reference` edges from `cref="..."`, so comment text like `// <see cref="Foo"/>` no longer pollutes `references` / `impact` with false compile-time dependencies while real `/// <see cref="Foo"/>` docs continue to work. Added a focused regression test that locks the plain-comment repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #646.
- **C# `using static` inherited nested-type rescue now canonicalizes constructed generic base type aliases (#734)** — `DbReader` now re-normalizes alias-expanded base references before resolving the containing type, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` keeps inherited nested-type pattern dependencies visible for both local and `global using` aliases such as `AliasBase = Probe.Base<int>`. Added focused CLI regressions for payload and `--count` coverage across local/global constructed-generic alias repros. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #734.
- **C# `using static` inherited nested-type rescue now resolves base classes through active type and namespace aliases (#733)** — `DbReader` now normalizes derived-type base references through active/local and project-wide `using Alias = ...;` mappings before walking the inherited nested-type rescue chain, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` keeps real derived-class pattern dependencies visible for both `class Derived : BaseAlias` and `class Derived : NsAlias.Base` when `Red` is inherited from the aliased base class. Added focused CLI regressions for both payload and `--count` coverage across type-alias and namespace-alias repros. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #733.
- **C# `using static` constant-pattern suppression now keeps multiline logical-pattern heads suppressed (#731)** — `DbReader` now reconstructs a small indexed multi-line context window before running the read-path `is` / `case` anchor scan, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` and `--count` no longer leak phantom `type_reference` rows from `value is` patterns where the imported constant heads (`Red or Blue`) continue on the next line. Added focused CLI regressions that pin both `Red` and `Blue` on the multiline logical-pattern repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #731.
- **C# `using static` inherited nested-type rescue no longer treats implemented interfaces as base-class scope (#730)** — `DbReader` now follows inherited nested-type rescue only through real C# base-class chains, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` no longer reports false-positive `type_reference` rows for `value is Red` / `typeof(Red)` when `Red` exists only as an implemented interface nested type. Added focused CLI regressions for both the result payload and `--count` path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #730.
- **C# `using static` same-name type rescue now keeps inherited protected nested-type patterns visible (#725)** — `DbReader` now treats base-class containing types as rescue candidates when the current C# scope is inside a derived type, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` no longer suppresses real pattern-site dependencies such as `value is Red` when `Red` is a protected nested type inherited from a base class. Added focused CLI regressions for the inherited protected nested-type repro and a derived-class constant-only control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #725.
- **C# `using static` constant-pattern suppression now keeps real same-name type references visible while still dropping imported constant heads (#682, #686, #692, #702, #703, #704, #705, #709, #717, #719)** — `DbReader` now filters C# pattern-position `type_reference` results through active `using static` imports plus the indexed enum/const-member containers, but it first checks the current C# scope for unqualified same-name type candidates so real type references such as `value is Red` (when `Red` is an actual in-scope type) are not suppressed. The read-path suppression now also consults the unclamped raw line text instead of the display-width-limited preview, so `references --max-line-width` cannot accidentally resurrect phantom matches that the full-width query correctly suppresses. Project-wide `global using static ...;` and `global using Namespace;` directives declared in other files are now included in the active import set for every C# file, while `using Alias = ...;` directives are no longer misread as namespace imports that rescue unrelated same-name types. Active C# type aliases now also block this suppression path, so `using Red = Probe.Real.Red;` keeps `value is Red` visible even when `using static` imported a constant named `Red`. The same-name type rescue now also respects C# `file` visibility, so a file-local `file class Red` in another file cannot make `value is Red` look like a real type dependency outside that file. Same-file `using Namespace;` imports likewise rescue real type heads, and the extractor now leaves ambiguous unqualified `using static` pattern heads in the DB for the workspace-aware read path instead of trying to pre-suppress them with file-local visibility only. That keeps fresh indexes correct when the real same-name type is declared in another file in the same namespace. Pattern-head detection on the read path now tokenizes backward through whitespace and block comments around `is` / `case`, so tabs and trivia-bearing forms like `value is/*comment*/Red` and `case\tRed:` stay suppressed instead of leaking phantom `type_reference` rows. `references --count` on this suppress path now streams raw rows instead of materializing every `ReferenceResult`, so count-only queries no longer pay for preview formatting. Cross-file constant/member cases such as `using static Probe.Color; value is Red or Blue;`, `case Red:`, and `case Red or Blue:` still stop surfacing phantom `type_reference` rows for `Red` / `Blue`, while genuine type heads like `Point` in `case Red or Point:`, same-file namespace-imported `Red`, and cross-file same-namespace `typeof(Red)` / `value is Red` remain visible. Added focused extractor regression coverage for qualified-vs-ambiguous logical-pattern syntax, CLI exact-name / `--count` regressions for the cross-file repros, and same-name real-type regressions that pin file-scoped-namespace, nested-type, same-file `using` namespace imports, same-namespace multi-file rescue, `global using`, alias-scope recovery, file-local visibility, and trivia-bearing constant patterns. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #682. Closes #686. Closes #692. Closes #702. Closes #703. Closes #704. Closes #705. Closes #709. Closes #717. Closes #719.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer leaks plain `//` or `////` comments as compile-time references (#723)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now gates `EmitCSharpDocCrefReferences(...)` on a real XML doc line check instead of merely looking for `cref="` anywhere in the raw source line. Ordinary comments such as `// <see cref="Foo"/>` and `//// <see cref="Foo"/>` no longer emit phantom `type_reference Foo` rows, while genuine `/// <see cref="Foo"/>` lines keep their documented-member container behavior. Added focused extractor regressions for plain-comment and four-slash controls alongside the existing real-doc control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #723.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction now keeps declaration-attached `/** ... */` documentation comments after the #723 plain-comment guard, without leaking post-`*/` same-line raw-string/code `cref` text, indexing ordinary block comments, misclassifying multiline string content as doc comments, letting method-body doc comments attach to the next declaration, dropping same-line declarations after `*/`, or shifting reference columns left on indented doc lines (#736, #831, #837, #839, #842, #846, #847)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now computes the effective doc-comment slice for each C# XML-doc line and runs `CSharpDocCrefRegex` only against that slice, while preserving `symbol_references.column` relative to the original physical source line. Real declaration-attached `/** ... */` documentation comments keep contributing dependency edges, including declarations that begin later on the same physical line after the closing `*/`; ordinary `//` / `////` comments, non-documenting block comments, and multiline raw/verbatim string content that merely starts with `/**` stay excluded; any `cref` text that appears after the closing `*/` on the same physical line is ignored; and method-body `///` / `/** ... */` comments no longer inherit the next later declaration as their documented container. Added focused extractor regressions for the `/** ... <see cref="Foo"/> ... */` repro, the mixed same-line raw-string follow-up, the same-line declaration-after-`*/` case, both `///` / `/** ... */` column-location contracts, multiline raw/verbatim string false-positive controls, and method-body negative controls for `///` / `/** ... */` even when a later declaration exists, and synchronized `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md` plus `CLAUDE.md` with the final contract. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #736. Closes #831. Closes #837. Closes #839. Closes #842. Closes #846. Closes #847.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer lets field-initializer lambda bodies document later members (#853)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now only lets XML-doc comments attach forward when the comment sits at namespace/type declaration depth, not merely anywhere inside a class-like container. That keeps `///` and `/** ... */` comments inside field-initializer lambdas and other nested executable blocks from leaking phantom `type_reference` rows onto later members such as `void Later() {}`. Added focused extractor regressions for both triple-slash and delimited XML-doc comments inside field-initializer lambdas. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #853.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer lets brace-free initializer continuations document later members (#868)** — The C# XML-doc forward-attachment guard now also rejects brace-free executable continuations at type scope, not just nested `{ ... }` blocks. Multiline field/property initializers and brace-free expression lambdas therefore stop leaking `///` / `/** ... */` `cref` rows onto later members such as `void Later() {}`. Added focused extractor regressions for brace-free field-initializer call arguments and brace-free expression lambdas in both triple-slash and delimited forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #868.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer lets top-level statements separate a doc comment from a later declaration (#877)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now refuses forward XML-doc attachment when any non-attribute executable code sits between the doc comment and the candidate declaration, which closes the top-level-statement gap where `/// <see cref="Foo"/>` or `/** ... */` above `System.Console.WriteLine(1);` could still leak a phantom `type_reference Foo` onto a later local function or type declaration. Added focused extractor regressions for triple-slash and delimited top-level-statement controls plus a later-type control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #877.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer skips past same-line non-target code after `*/` (#887)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now blocks same-line forward attachment when the text after a delimited XML-doc close contains real code or a non-symbol declaration instead of only attributes leading into the documented declaration. This closes the remaining false positive where `/** ... */ string text = "";` on one line could still attach the `cref` to a later `void Run() {}` on the next line, while keeping the valid `/** ... */ [Attr]` then next-line declaration shape working. Added focused extractor regressions for both the negative field-then-method repro and the positive attribute-then-method control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #887.
- **C# verbatim type names in pattern/type-keyword references no longer hang or disappear (#677)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats `@not` / `@default` as legal escaped identifiers in C# type-pattern and type-keyword scanners instead of collapsing them into bare pattern tokens or stalling on the leading `@`. `value is @not`, `case @default:`, and `typeof(@default)` now emit normalized `type_reference` rows (`not` / `default`) while bare constant patterns such as `is not null` and `case default:` still stay filtered. Added focused extractor and CLI exact-name regressions for both escaped-type and bare-token control cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #677.
- **C# type-test references now distinguish `is not` patterns from constant `case Enum.Member` labels without dropping recursive/logical/positional `case Type ...` patterns (#645, #647, #661, #666, #668, #669, #670, #671, #672, #673, #674, #675)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now splits C# type-test handling between `is` / `is not` / `as` expressions and a small structural `case` pattern scanner. This restores the real `Foo` dependency for `x is not Foo`, suppresses phantom constant-pattern rows such as `type_reference not` / `type_reference null`, stops `value is Color.Red or Color.Blue`, `case Color.Red:`, `case Namespace.Color.Red:`, `case Color.Red or Namespace.Color.Blue:`, and `case ErrorCodes.NotFound or ErrorCodes.Forbidden:` from being misclassified as compile-time type dependencies, and still keeps legitimate `case Point:`, `case Point when value != null:`, `case ILogger logger:`, `case Point { X: 0, Y: 0 }:`, `case Point(var x, var y):`, `case Point or null:`, `case not Point:`, `case Probe.Point or null:`, `case not Probe.Point:`, `case global::Probe.Point or null:`, `case N2.Color.Red r:`, `case Outer.Red or Outer.Blue:`, `case Point or Shape:`, `case Color.Red or Point:`, and `case Foo @or:` type references intact, including later genuine heads inside logical patterns. Added focused extractor regressions plus CLI-level multi-file and mixed-logical-pattern regressions for the reported issue repros and constant-pattern / logical-pattern / verbatim-designator / nested-type follow-ups. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #645, #647, #661, #666, #668, #669, #670, #671, #672, #673, #674, #675.
- **C# carried multiline string continuations no longer hide later same-line type declarations (#630, #633)** — `SymbolExtractor` now skips the top-level `"` / `;` / `}` tokens that can appear before the next real C# declaration when a physical line starts by closing a carried verbatim/raw string or a same-line container. The carried-state same-line restart fix now also flows through accessor-event sibling recovery, so `event E { add { } remove { } }` no longer blocks later nested/outer type siblings on the same close-line. Added focused extractor regressions for carried verbatim-string and raw-string continuation lines, including accessor-event variants. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #630, #633.
- **C# same-line nested type signatures no longer absorb later outer siblings (#635)** — `SymbolExtractor` now resolves same-line C# brace-bodied signature endpoints in the carried lexical-state collapsed line domain before translating back to raw columns, so continuation-line close tokens no longer make a nested type such as `Child` swallow the later outer sibling `OuterChild` into its stored signature. The carried verbatim/raw continuation regressions now also assert the exact `Child` / `OuterChild` signatures. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #635.
- **C#/Java type-position identifiers now produce `type_reference` edges (#256)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now indexes C#/Java base lists, declaration types, parameter/return types, generic constraints, Java `throws`, C# `is` / `as`, Java `instanceof`, and C# XML-doc `cref` sites as compile-time dependency edges. This lets `references` and `impact` see rename-sensitive type dependencies such as `class Derived : Base, ILogger`, `Base parent`, `List<ILogger>`, `where T : ILogger`, `throws IOException`, and `<see cref="Base.Do"/>` without polluting the default `callers` / `callees` dynamic call graph. Added focused extractor regressions for the C# and Java issue repros and updated the developer/AI guidance docs to document the expanded `type_reference` contract. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #256.
- **C# explicit-interface events now keep the trailing event name instead of disappearing from the index (#351)** — `SymbolExtractor` now has a dedicated C# explicit-interface event row, so declarations such as `event EventHandler IFoo.Evt { add { } remove { } }` are indexed as `Evt` under the implementing container instead of being omitted from `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline`. The dedicated row shares the existing explicit-interface qualifier contract, so same-line and next-line accessor blocks and generic interface qualifiers stay aligned with the method/property explicit-member paths. Added focused extractor regression coverage for multiline accessor blocks, same-line accessor blocks, generic qualifiers, and phantom-name guards. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #351.
- **Empty same-line C# nested type bodies no longer swallow later sibling types or closing-line outer properties (#585)** — `SymbolExtractor` now refuses to treat an empty compact class-like body as an internal restart target and reopens the full same-line pattern list for later siblings after compact class-like members, so fixtures such as `public interface I<T1, T2> { } public class Sibling { }` and `public class Child { } } public int P { get; set; }` still expose the later sibling declaration instead of stopping at the nested type's empty body. Added focused extractor regressions for the later-sibling-type and later-outer-property shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #585.
- **C# verbatim identifiers now survive symbol and reference extraction (#367, #626, #627, #628)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts `@`-prefixed C# declaration names across the affected declaration rows and normalizes stored symbol names to their semantic identifier (`@class` → `class`), including qualified namespace/import/container names such as `Outer.@class` and conversion-operator target types such as `implicit operator List<@class>`. `ReferenceExtractor` now accepts verbatim call / constructor / parenless-initializer targets without letting keyword ignore lists swallow legal calls like `@if()`. The exact-name stale-DB contract version also advances so older DBs with pre-fix canonical names degrade explicitly instead of silently returning false negatives. Added focused extractor and exact-name query regressions for normalized symbol names, qualified namespace/import lookups, normalized conversion-operator names, stale canonical-name DB detection, and normalized `call` / `instantiate` edges. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #367, #626, #627, #628.
- **C# parenthesized LINQ clause keywords no longer break query range-variable scope tracking (#564, #606, #607, #608, #609, #610, #612, #614, #615, #617, #621, #622, #623)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now recognizes legal parenthesized `select(` / `group(` / `orderby(` query clauses while keeping the top-level clause scanner anchored to real clause boundaries, so range-variable shadow scopes no longer survive past `select(`/`group(` or stay stuck in nested subqueries after `orderby(`. The follow-up guard now also rejects operator-adjacent and contextual-prefix local function calls such as `items.Count() > select(items)`, `< select(items)`, `! select(items)`, `await select(items)`, `throw select(items)` / `throw group(items)`, cast-close local calls like `(object)select(items)`, `(CustomType)select(items)`, `(customType)select(items)`, and parenthesized keyword-named value expressions like `(Select)` when they are not real casts, and split-line `select\n(items)` / `group\n(items)` while still allowing real clause boundaries after postfix null-forgiving, nullable-cast suffixes including array-rank forms, postfix increment, generic-close contexts like `Sink.Maybe(Status)! select(...)`, `value as Status? select(...)`, `value as Status[,]? select(...)`, `counter++ select(...)`, and `List<int> select(...)`, plus non-cast parenthesized `orderby` expressions such as `(flag ? left : right)`, `(left ?? right)`, and `(Demo.Status.Ready)` before `select(...)`. Added extractor and CLI `references --exact-name --json` / `inspect --exact-name --json` regressions for the reported repros, the operator/contextual follow-ups, postfix null-forgiving control, nullable/postfix controls, cast-close controls, generic-close control, split-line controls, simple-identifier and alias-cast controls, keyword-named value controls, qualified-member controls, and ternary/coalesce controls. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #564, #606, #607, #608, #609, #610, #612, #614, #615, #617, #621, #622, #623.
- **Kotlin anonymous companion objects now default to `Companion` instead of emitting blank symbol names (#315)** — `SymbolExtractor` now normalizes unnamed `companion object { ... }` declarations to the Kotlin-default symbol name `Companion`, so `symbols`, `outline`, and `inspect` no longer surface empty-name rows and nested members such as `const val` / `fun` attach to `Companion` instead of an empty container. A defensive empty-name guard also blocks future blank symbol rows from entering the symbol list. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #315.
- **C# same-line header-line auto-properties now survive multiline type bodies (#580)** — `SymbolExtractor` now restarts the C# same-line pattern scan from just after a type header's opening `{` even when the enclosing `class` / `struct` / `interface` body closes on a later line. Hybrid shapes such as `public class Outer { public int P { get; }` + next-line `}` no longer stop after indexing only the outer type, so `symbols`, `definition`, `outline`, and `inspect` now keep the first member that shares the header line. Added focused extractor regressions for the `class`, `struct`, and `interface` variants and guards against phantom `property Outer`-style rows. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #580.
- **Brace-bodied last inner members in wrapped C# nested types no longer hide closing-brace-line outer siblings (#554)** — `SymbolExtractor` now uses the non-closing same-line restart helper when a compact C# type header (`Child { }`) is rejected as a false brace-property candidate, so the recovery path skips the intervening container-closing `}` and still reaches later outer siblings on the same line. Wrapped fixtures like `public partial class Child { } } public int Q { get; }` and `... } public partial class Sibling` now keep `Child` under `Wrapped` while preserving the outer property/class sibling for `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline`. Added focused extractor regressions for both the outer-property and outer-type sibling shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #554.
- **C# switch-expression declaration-pattern guard parsing now also ignores `when` inside multiline block comments before `=>` (#597)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now blanks C# block-comment bodies and delimiters, so the switch-expression arm-preparation path no longer lets a multiline `/* ... when ... */` leak into `FindTopLevelCSharpWhenKeywordOffset()`. This closes the remaining false enum-member `call` regression from #583/#596 when a declaration-pattern arm keeps its real guard after a multiline comment, and adds extractor plus CLI `references --exact-name --json` coverage for the multiline repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/StructuralLineMasker.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #597.
- **C# switch-expression declaration-pattern guard parsing now ignores `when` inside arm trivia before `=>` (#596)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now masks and prepares the switch-expression arm text before it tries to recover declaration-pattern designations, so block comments, line comments, and other non-code trivia can no longer trick the top-level `when` scan into truncating `Holder Status /* when comment */ => ...` as if a guard had started. This keeps the declaration-pattern value receiver alive and prevents the #583 false enum-member `call` edge from reappearing when inline comments contain `when`. Added extractor and CLI `inspect --exact-name --json` regressions for the comment-bearing repro while preserving the earlier guard/no-guard coverage. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #596.
- **C# switch-expression declaration-pattern guards now keep pattern-variable shadow scopes alive (#583)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now recognizes switch-expression declaration patterns like `Holder Status when ... =>` before trimming any top-level `when` guard, so guard-side member access such as `Status.Ready` no longer leaks a false enum-member `call` edge while real `Demo.Status.Ready` hits in the arm body and fallback arms still survive. Added extractor regressions for both the `when`-guard repro and a no-guard control, plus CLI `references --exact-name --json` coverage for the reported case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #583.
- **C# query generic-comma scope tracking now keeps generic `as` null comparisons inside the selector scope (#591)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats `==` / `!=` immediately after a balanced generic close as valid terminal-selector continuations, so selectors such as `select Status as Dictionary<int, int> != null ? Status.Ready : 0` no longer drop the LINQ range-variable shadow scope at the `>` token.
